data.title: Head of Architectural Integration, Building Safety & Envelope
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
**What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?**
 At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates ne
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?**
 At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.
   

  

 WSP is seeking to appoint a Head of Architecture Integration \& Building Performance, a role that provides a single point of portfolio\-level direction and leadership across Fire \& Life Safety, Building Enclosures / Facades, and Technical Architecture, with clear line management accountability for the Heads of these disciplines.
   

  

 The Heads of Discipline already deliver strongly and cover many of the technical and operational activities within their teams. This role is not created to duplicate that work. It is created to formalize the portfolio\-level direction and alignment that has historically been driven by the Director of Specialists, ensuring consistent prioritization, governance, and follow\-through across the three business lines.
   

  

 The role will drive disciplined implementation of the Specialist Services Strategy and the Global Specialist Strategy for this portfolio, with particular emphasis on accelerating growth in Technical Architecture (architecture\-led technical coordination and expanded scope), while proactively managing delivery risk where FLS and building enclosures performance can impact project outcomes.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Portfolio leadership \& direction (non\-negotiable)**
* Set one clear direction across the portfolio: priorities, standards, decision\-making, and escalation routes.
* Align discipline plans so delivery and growth efforts are coordinated and outcome\-driven.
* Lead delivery recovery when projects are off track due to late inputs, rework, inconsistent advice, or weak coordination, with particular focus on high\-risk interfaces (e.g., Envelope ↔ Architecture/Structure/MEP/FLS dependencies).


**Line management of Heads of Discipline**
* Line\-manage the Heads of FLS, Building Enclosures/Facades, and Technical Architecture with accountability for performance, delivery discipline, team health, and capability building.
* Coach and enable the Heads of Discipline on complex delivery issues, client risk, resourcing constraints, and cross\-discipline coordination.
* Drive an operating rhythm that reduces reliance on the Director of Specialists for day\-to\-day direction.


**Technical authority \& delivery governance (quality \+ consistency)**
* Act as a credible technical authority (in at least one of the three disciplines) with the ability to challenge and guide across the portfolio.
* Define “what good looks like” for deliverables (minimum content, evidence requirements, review expectations) and ensure consistent application across the portfolio.
* Implement and enforce governance so issues do not repeat: standards, peer reviews, readiness gates, decision logs, and action\-closure discipline.


**Operational Excellence (ways of working that stick)**
* Establish consistent delivery ways of working across the portfolio (review gates, interfaces, escalation routes) and drive adoption.
* Drive measurable improvement in right\-first\-time outputs, fewer late changes, and clearer accountability across the three disciplines.


**Strategy adherence \& implementation (Specialist Services Strategy 2025–2027\)**
* Own implementation across the four pillars for this portfolio: Clients, Expertise, Operational Excellence, People \& Culture.
* Ensure discipline pillar champions/leads are active and contributing as designed, and translate strategy into practical execution and consistent follow\-through.
* Remove blockers so progress is sustained and not dependent on ad hoc senior intervention.


**Go\-to\-market and growth leadership (per business line)**
* Drive growth of each business line through market positioning, bid quality, cross\-sell, and consistent go\-to\-market collateral aligned to the Global Specialist Strategy intent.
* For Technical Architecture, accelerate growth by expanding scope and strengthening architecture\-led technical coordination and design integration aligned to the team’s published strategy direction.
* Contribute to global alignment outputs relevant to this portfolio (e.g., discipline forums / collaboration inputs and go\-to\-market strategy contributions) as required.


**People leadership \& capability building (portfolio bench strength)**
* Build bench strength across the portfolio (deputies, succession, coaching) and embed capability uplift through People \& Culture and Expertise pillars.
* Identify targeted upskilling and recruitment priorities aligned to strategy and growth needs.


**Qualifications**
* 20\+ years’ experience with senior leadership exposure in complex building environments, with strong relevance to Architecture / Technical Architecture and the ability to lead across Building Safety and Envelope disciplines.
* Demonstrated ability to lead senior discipline heads and drive consistent standards and outcomes across multiple specialisms.
* Track record of implementing operating models and standards (not just writing strategy).
* Strong stakeholder influence across disciplines and regions.


**Essential Behaviours**
* Delivery spine: drives closure, makes decisions stick, holds standards under pressure.
* Strategic thinking with practical execution: converts intent into clear prioritisation and outcomes.
* Collaborative “one team” mindset aligned to global specialist connectivity and growth objectives.


**Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.**
 Join our close\-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world.
   

  

**With us, you can. Apply today.**

 management, identifying opportunities and client development via CRM and services initiatives
   

  

 and operations.

 team and HR.

 recommendations/action plan.

 A creator, artisan and seller of high\-quality objects since 1837, Hermès is an independent, family\-owned French house that employs nearly 25,185 people worldwide. data.title: Ops. Integration Manager (UAE), Hub Delivery
data.raw_snippet:
**Description**
 At Amazon, we are working to be the most customer\-centric company on earth, striving to provide best\-in\-class delivery services. Our goal is to ensure that all customers, no matter where they live, can get their orders as quickly, accurately, and cost\-effectively as possible.
data.jd_text:
**Description**
 At Amazon, we are working to be the most customer\-centric company on earth, striving to provide best\-in\-class delivery services. Our goal is to ensure that all customers, no matter where they live, can get their orders as quickly, accurately, and cost\-effectively as possible.
   

  

 Our last\-mile delivery team is seeking a dynamic, autonomous, and well\-organized Operations Integration Manager for the Hub Delivery program. This role will drive integration and standardization of Hub Delivery processes across the UAE AMZL Network, partnering with Country ACES, SnOP, Central Operations, AMZL Operations, Product teams, and other key stakeholders.
   

  

 This is an opportunity to be part of a rapidly growing environment focused on improving the delivery experience for our customers while driving operational excellence and cost efficiency.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 Design, model, and implement new operations processes for Hub Delivery across the UAE AMZL Network, ensuring scalability and alignment with network capacity planning.
   

  

 Drive improvement initiatives for existing processes to meet cost, quality, and customer experience goals, using data\-driven insights to identify and prioritize opportunities.
   

  

 Gather and analyze operational data to establish network\-wide standards in partnership with cross\-functional teams, ensuring consistency and best practice adoption across all delivery stations.
   

  

 Build strong partnerships with operations field leaders and stakeholders (Central Operations, SnOP, DSP teams, Country ACES) to understand improvement opportunities and remove operational barriers.
   

  

 Analyze and solve operational blockers with a focus on root cause analysis, driving forward sustainable solutions that improve network performance and partner experience.
   

  

 Innovate to continuously improve customer experience while removing cost and complexity from the fulfillment network through process optimization and automation.
   

  

 Partner with Product teams to conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT), review launch plans, pilot new features, and manage full\-scale rollouts across the network with clear success metrics.
   

  

 Represent UAE Hub Delivery operations in regional and global forums, providing insights, performance updates, and strategic recommendations to senior leadership.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5\+ years of experience defining and implementing process improvement initiatives using data and metrics in operations or supply chain environments.
* Advanced knowledge of Excel (Pivot Tables, VLookUps, complex formulas) and experience with data visualization tools for operational reporting.
* Proven ability using data and metrics to determine root causes, drive improvements, and measure impact of process changes.
* Experience working cross\-functionally with technical and non\-technical teams, including operations, product, and engineering stakeholders.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with ability to translate complex operational challenges into actionable solutions.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* 3\+ years of program or project management experience, including managing multiple concurrent initiatives with competing priorities.
* Demonstrated experience driving end\-to\-end delivery of complex projects and communicating results to senior leadership with clear business impact.
* Track record of driving measurable process improvements in last\-mile delivery, logistics, or fulfillment operations.
* Strong stakeholder management skills with experience influencing multiple stakeholders at varied levels of the organization without direct authority.
* Experience building processes from ground up, including project management frameworks, standard operating procedures, and implementation schedules.
* Experience with SQL, Tableau, or other business intelligence tools for operational analytics.
* Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Operations Management, Supply Chain, or related field; advanced degree is a plus.


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 \- Q\-Express Documents Transport

data.jd_text:
**Position Overview**
 We are seeking a highly organized, detail\-oriented, and dependable
 **Junior Project Manager** 
 to support our expanding remote operations team. This role plays a key part in coordinating projects and workflows across regional teams in the MENA region, including the UAE, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia.
   

  

 This is an
 **entry\-level position** 
 ideal for individuals looking to build a strong foundation in project coordination, operations management, workflow optimization, and cross\-functional collaboration. The ideal candidate thrives in structured environments, values precision, and takes ownership of keeping projects organized and on track.
   

  

 You will contribute to the successful delivery of projects by maintaining accurate documentation, tracking progress, coordinating tasks, and ensuring alignment across teams. Your work will directly support operational efficiency and timely project execution.
   

  

 No prior professional experience is required—
 **full training and structured guidance will be provided** 
 .
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Project Coordination \& Execution**
* Assist in planning, organizing, and tracking project timelines and deliverables
* Break down project tasks and ensure clear assignment to team members
* Monitor progress and ensure tasks are completed on schedule
* Follow up on delays, blockers, or dependencies
* Support project managers in day\-to\-day coordination activities


**Task \& Workflow Management**
* Review incoming project\-related requests and prioritize accordingly
* Assign and route tasks to appropriate stakeholders
* Track task progress from initiation to completion
* Maintain clear documentation of task status and updates
* Ensure workflows follow defined processes and standards


**Data Management \& Documentation**
* Maintain and update project trackers (Google Sheets / Excel / project tools)
* Ensure data accuracy, completeness, and consistency
* Organize project documentation using structured systems
* Maintain logs of updates, changes, and key project actions
* Identify and resolve inconsistencies or missing information


**Reporting \& Communication**
* Prepare regular project status updates and summaries
* Communicate progress, risks, and issues clearly to stakeholders
* Identify recurring challenges or inefficiencies
* Escalate risks or delays when necessary
* Support internal reporting with accurate and organized data


**Scheduling \& Team Coordination**
* Assist with scheduling meetings, deadlines, and milestones
* Track team availability and project timelines
* Send reminders for upcoming deadlines and deliverables
* Support onboarding coordination and checklist tracking


**Quality Assurance \& Process Improvement**
* Review project outputs for accuracy and completeness
* Ensure compliance with internal processes and SOPs
* Maintain consistency in documentation and reporting
* Identify opportunities to improve workflows and efficiency
* Support updates to internal documentation and procedures


**Performance Expectations**
* Maintain high levels of accuracy and attention to detail
* Ensure projects and tasks stay organized and on schedule
* Communicate clearly and professionally across teams
* Proactively follow up on pending or unclear items
* Demonstrate accountability in a remote work environment
* Adhere to structured processes and workflows


**Required Qualifications**
* Basic proficiency in Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel
* Strong written communication skills
* Strong organizational and time management abilities
* Ability to follow processes and instructions consistently
* Comfortable working with structured workflows and repetitive tasks
* Ability to work independently in a remote environment
* Reliable internet connection and computer access
* Entry\-level candidates welcome (training provided)


**Work Details**
* Location: Fully Remote
* Region Supported: MENA Region (UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia)
* Schedule: Flexible, with consistent availability required
* Hours: Full\-time or part\-time depending on workload
* Compensation: Based on experience and engagement


**Ideal Candidate Profile**
* Starting a career in project management or operations
* Enjoys organizing tasks, timelines, and workflows
* Detail\-oriented and process\-driven
* Strong sense of accountability and ownership
* Interested in long\-term remote career growth


**Growth \& Career Development**
**You Will Gain Hands\-on Experience In**
* Project coordination and delivery
* Workflow and task management systems
* Stakeholder communication and reporting


**Potential Career Progression Paths**
* Project Manager
* Operations Coordinator
* Program Coordinator
* Workflow Analyst
* Team Lead
* Operations Manager


**Why Join Us?**
* 100% remote work environment
* Entry\-level friendly with full training provided
* Structured processes and clear expectations
* Supportive and growth\-focused team culture
* Exposure to regional MENA operations
* Long\-term career development opportunities


**Job Requirements Summary**
* Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
* Clear written communication
* Ability to manage tasks and timelines effectively
* Basic spreadsheet proficiency
* Self\-motivated and reliable in a remote setting

data.title: Junior Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
At Gulf Associates, project work depends on more than plans and deadlines. It depends on someone noticing what is moving, what is stuck, what needs clarification, and what needs to happen next.
   

 We are hiring a Junior Project Manager for a remote role focused on coordination, follow\-through, a
data.jd_text:
At Gulf Associates, project work depends on more than plans and deadlines. It depends on someone noticing what is moving, what is stuck, what needs clarification, and what needs to happen next.
   

 We are hiring a Junior Project Manager for a remote role focused on coordination, follow\-through, and steady execution. This is a good fit for someone who is organized, practical, and comfortable helping work move across teams without turning every task into unnecessary process.
   

 This role is not about sitting above the work and speaking in abstractions. It is about staying close enough to the details to keep projects on track. You would help structure tasks, monitor progress, follow up with the right people, keep records current, and make sure communication does not break down between moving parts.
   

 Because our work involves clients, timelines, and distributed teams, this role suits someone who can bring order without becoming rigid. Good judgment matters here. So does consistency.
   

**What the work actually looks like**
 On one day, you might be updating a project tracker, checking whether a deadline is at risk, and following up with team members so next steps are clear. On another, you may be preparing notes from a meeting, organizing open items, confirming responsibilities, and making sure a client request has been properly translated into internal action.
   

 You may also help identify where a process is slowing things down, where ownership is unclear, or where a small gap in coordination could later create a larger delivery issue.
   

 You will not be expected to run complex programs from the start. You will be expected to pay attention, stay organized, communicate clearly, and help keep projects moving in a reliable way.
   

**What the work involves** 
* Helping coordinate tasks, timelines, and follow\-ups across active projects
* Keeping project records, notes, trackers, and status updates organized and current
* Following up on open items so work does not lose momentum
* Supporting internal communication between different contributors and functions
* Helping prepare meeting summaries, action points, and next\-step tracking
* Noticing when something is unclear, delayed, or at risk of being missed
* Contributing to a more structured and dependable delivery process


**You are likely a strong fit if you** 
* Communicate clearly and professionally in English
* Are organized and good at keeping track of details across multiple moving tasks
* Can follow process, but also know when something needs escalation or clarification
* Are comfortable working remotely without needing constant supervision
* Stay calm and methodical when priorities compete for attention
* Like being dependable, prepared, and easy to work with
* Can help bring structure to work without making things heavier than they need to be


**Background**
 Previous experience in project coordination, operations, administration, client service, team support, or similar work can be helpful, but we are also open to candidates earlier in their career who show strong organizational ability and professional judgment.
   

 You may have built those skills through work, internships, university projects, freelance support, or other practical experience. What matters most is that you can help keep real work organized, visible, and moving.
   

**What the role offers** 
* Fully remote work
* Exposure to real project coordination in an international working environment
* Practical responsibility connected to active business work
* A role with clear value in day\-to\-day delivery
* A professional setting where reliability, structure, and communication matter
* An opportunity to strengthen project judgment through hands\-on coordination work


 This role is well suited to someone who wants to build a career in project management through real responsibility, steady involvement, and work that directly supports delivery rather than sitting around it.

data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose** 




 The Assistant Project Manager will work in conjunction with Operations, Design Department, Procurement, Supply Chain, MIS, Real Estate, Lease Admin and General Admin to construct world class restaurants with optimal quality, time and cost. This role has a direct impact on New Store Openings, Remodels and Capex.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Liaise with Landlords, developers, Development department and establish project design parameters.
* Prepare Preliminary Site Survey with detailed project construction and design requirements and constraints.
* Liaise with Design Team during the Project design stage.
* Evaluate Site, Prepare Detailed FS, Review B.O.Q. and Assign vendors.
* Scout and Evaluate New vendors.
* Liaise with contractors or outside consultants and obtain building permits.
* Prepare Construction Program and timeline.
* Start construction and follow up with requisite site visits.
* Approved Materials for Contractors based on Contracts, B.O.Q. specification.
* Liaise with Design team and ensure project is executed as per Design parameters.
* Prepare signage, furniture, and all other owner provided packages by liaisoning with approved Sub\-contractors.
* Assess Interim payments and prepare Final payment and additional/variation works.
* Complete Construction and hand\-over.
* Prepare Snag list and complete snags.
* Follow\-up maintenance period snags and complaints and thereafter release retention money on successful completion.



  





**Education \& Experience** 



* Bachelor’s degree in engineering/architecture
* 7\+ years experience with the FMCG / Retail / Restaurant Industry



  





**Knowledge** 



* Ability to plan, organise and adapt in various situations to meet business objectives.
* Strong understanding of building codes, accessibility standards, and life safety regulations.
* Knowledge of F\&B or retail specific design considerations (kitchen layouts, display areas, customer flow) is an essential.
* Knowledge of materials, finishes, and construction methods specific to F\&B or retail environments (a must).
* Strong project management skills.



  





**Skills** 



* Exceptional presentation skills.
* Ability to meet deadlines.
* Ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects.
* Strong organizational skills
* High\-quality work ethics without supervision
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Ability to solve problems by drawing upon the collective thinking of the broader team through design solutions.
* Strong leadership skills
* Client relationship management skills.

data.title: Head of Agency | Marketing Director | Customer Success
data.raw_snippet:
Ecomma is a rapidly growing private equity firm with proven expertise in acquiring ecommerce brands worldwide. We excel in a range of areas that drive genuine results and online growth, including:
 


* **Value Creation** 
 : Strategically investing to maximize the potential of our acquired brands.
data.jd_text:
Ecomma is a rapidly growing private equity firm with proven expertise in acquiring ecommerce brands worldwide. We excel in a range of areas that drive genuine results and online growth, including:
 


* **Value Creation** 
 : Strategically investing to maximize the potential of our acquired brands.
* **Operational Excellence** 
 : Leveraging best practices and a seasoned team to optimize brand operations.
* **Sustainable Growth** 
 : Prioritizing long\-term success with sustainable practices.
* **Data\-driven Marketing Strategies** 
 : Using data to tailor marketing campaigns for optimal reach and conversion.



  





 We asked our current team members to tell us what they love about working at Ecomma, and here's what they shared:
 


* Supporting
 **top\-tier brands** 
 that truly make a difference in the world.
* Knowing the impact of our
 **expertise** 
 (check out our success stories).
* Connecting with
 **C\-level executives** 
 and leading e\-commerce businesses.
* Working in the world's most progressive industry at a firm that prioritizes
 **personal development.**
* Enjoying a
 **great atmosphere** 
 with fun team outings.
* Having the opportunity for
 **substantial compensation, including lucrative bonuses.**
* As
 **Head of Agency \| Marketing Director \| Customer Success** 
 , you will be the driving force behind the operational performance of Ecomma's active portfolio businesses and post\-sale agency accounts. Your mission is simple: build and run a tight operating system that stabilises businesses quickly, drives revenue and EBITDA improvement, makes portfolio businesses exit\-ready, and ensures the engine behind both Portfolio and Agency runs with discipline, speed, and accountability.



  





 This is a highly commercial and turnaround\-focused role. You will take ownership of portfolio business performance, agency operations oversight, transition and handover discipline, EBITDA improvement, and Build Your Own project coordination, with the clear goal of turning around the businesses we manage and making them commercially stronger, more stable, and less founder\-dependent. You will work closely with our COO, CEO and leadership team to ensure operations directly support commercial outcomes, retention, and exit readiness. We are looking for a Head of Agency \| Marketing Director \| Customer Success with a strong operator background in e\-commerce, portfolio management, or multi\-brand business operations. Someone who understands revenue, margin, and EBITDA, how to identify where a business is leaking, how to move fast, and how to use AI tools and workflows to run a lean and highly effective operating department. Are you the next Head of Agency ready to scale Ecomma to the next level?
 



  





 What you will do
 



  




* **Own Portfolio Business Performance** 
 : Take full ownership of the weekly operating rhythm across all active portfolio businesses. Track revenue, gross margin, and EBITDA by business, identify underperforming brands early, and drive recovery plans that push businesses toward stronger commercial performance and exit readiness.
* **Drive Agency Operations Oversight** 
 : Oversee the operating side of agency\-managed businesses after handover. Ensure clear alignment between what has been promised and what is realistically deliverable, with tight coordination between CSM, performance, ops, and technical support. Improve account stability, issue resolution speed, and internal accountability to support retention.
* **Own Transition and Handover Discipline:** 
 Own and enforce seller\-to\-portfolio and portfolio\-to\-agency handover standards. Ensure all required inputs, documents, and business context are transferred properly, flag incomplete handovers early, and reduce loss of momentum during change of ownership and internal transition.
* **Drive EBITDA and Self\-Sustainability:** 
 Push portfolio businesses toward stronger EBITDA performance. Improve visibility into what is driving profitability or dragging it down, support working capital awareness and operational cost discipline, and help the division move toward being financially healthier and less dependent on founder intervention.
* **Oversee Build Your Own Projects:** 
 Lead the operational coordination of active BYO projects once live. Ensure structured follow\-through across launch, setup, and performance phases, and coordinate with relevant teams to keep projects moving and commercially on track.
* **Solve Cross\-Functional Operating Issues:** 
 Work with internal teams to resolve supplier, logistics, customer service, marketing, and website issues affecting portfolio performance. Be the person who unblocks things quickly so senior leadership does not need to jump in constantly.
* **Make Portfolio Businesses Exit\-Ready:** 
 Drive brands toward stronger commercial performance, operational stability, and sellability so portfolio numbers become visible, reliable, and improving.
* **Build a High\-Performance Operating Team:** 
 Set a clear weekly cadence for priorities, blockers, follow\-ups, and accountability. Ensure team members know what they own and by when, push for strong follow\-through rather than just updates, and escalate issues early rather than allowing drift.
* **Implement AI\-First Workflows:** 
 Bring an AI\-first mindset to the operating department by using AI tools, automations, and systems to improve speed, output, reporting, and team efficiency across portfolio and agency.
* **Track Performance Relentlessly:** 
 Set clear KPIs and reporting structures across portfolio and agency, including portfolio revenue growth, EBITDA, number of exit\-ready businesses, time to stabilise post\-handover, red/at\-risk brand count, recovery rate on underperforming brands, handover completion quality, and operational SLA follow\-through. You understand that operations exist to generate measurable business outcomes, not activity reports.
* **Improve Stability and Accountability:** 
 Continuously refine operating rhythm, escalation paths, and cross\-functional discipline so agency ops become less reactive and more structured, and businesses stop drifting after handover.
* **Create a Scalable Operating Engine:** 
 Build an operating function that is structured, repeatable, lean, and able to scale alongside the company as we continue to grow.



  





 We operate using Google Workspace, Slack, and Notion. Thus, familiarity and hands\-on experience with these platforms are important for this role. The candidate should be fluent in English, with excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills. A strong commercial instinct, analytical thinking, fast execution, and high ownership are essential. Tools in use may include portfolio performance trackers, P\&L and EBITDA reporting dashboards, handover and documentation systems, project and task management platforms, client/account management tools, and AI platforms that improve output, speed, and operating efficiency.
 



  





 What you bring to the table:
 


* 5\+ years of experience in e\-commerce operations, portfolio management, multi\-brand business operations, or agency operations leadership, ideally in a high\-growth, roll\-up, or aggregator environment.
* Proven experience stabilising, turning around, and driving commercial improvement across multiple businesses at the same time.
* A strong background in operations and commercial performance, with clear evidence that you know how to move businesses from underperforming to exit\-ready.
* Experience leading and coordinating cross\-functional teams, including CSM, performance, ops, finance, and client/account contributors.
* A highly commercial mindset. You understand revenue, gross margin, and EBITDA, not just task execution, and you know how operations should directly support commercial outcomes.
* Experience working in e\-commerce operations, roll\-up/aggregator/multi\-brand portfolios, agency operations, or GM/multi\-store environments is a strong plus.
* Strong analytical skills and confidence with P\&L, reporting, attribution, and decision\-making based on data.
* An AI\-first mindset. You actively use AI tools, workflows, and automations to improve operating output, team efficiency, and speed of execution.
* Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple businesses, handovers, and priorities at the same time.
* Strong follow\-through, escalation, and cross\-functional coordination skills. Able to work with performance, ops, finance, and client/account teams without getting lost in the noise.
* A hands\-on mentality. You are strategic, but you are also willing to dig into the detail, fix issues directly, and drive accountability when needed.
* Calm under pressure, but not soft on accountability. High standards, strong ownership, and the ability to build a culture of urgency and operational clarity.
* Fluent in English with strong verbal and written communication skills.
* High ethical standards and a commitment to conducting business with integrity.



  





 The position will be filled immediately for the right candidate.
 



  





 Working at Ecomma is more than just a job. If you believe in seizing the present to enhance your future, you're in the right place. Combining hard work with the right atmosphere is our golden formula. We don't operate with a 9\-6 mindset, allowing you to manage your own working hours. We've found this approach yields the best results. Ecomma: a private equity firm you can count on.
 



  





 Please note: Proficiency in English is required.
 



  





 Does this opportunity excite you? Are you eager to dive in and unleash your creative finance mind? If you want to be a part of the Ecomma team, send your motivation letter and CV addressed to the respective job title application.
 



  





*If this role isn't a fit for you but you know someone it might suit, please share it with them \- we'd greatly appreciate it!*

data.title: Director Project Control

We are uniquely resourced with end\-to\-end services to take clients from inspiration – through conceptualization – to realization of planning, design or project delivery in the built and natural environments. For over 60 years we’ve l
data.jd_text:
KEO is a creative enterprise, where innovation is a way of life. We are uniquely resourced with end\-to\-end services to take clients from inspiration – through conceptualization – to realization of planning, design or project delivery in the built and natural environments. For over 60 years we’ve led with vision, contributing to many of the world’s most ambitious projects, iconic places, remarkable experiences and prosperous communities.
 



  





 As a highly integrated and agile AEP/PMCM firm, KEO is recognized by ENR as one of the Top 225 International Design Firms and one of the Top 20 International PM/CM Firms. We are also ranked by World Architecture as the 51st largest global architecture firm and the \#1 Firm in the Middle East Region in their 2024 WA100 Survey.
 



  





 We invite you to join us. Why?
 



  





 When you join KEO, you’ll discover more than just a job – you’ll find a supportive environment that fosters your professional development through internal global mobility and career development and does so within a culture that supports company\-wide health and well\-being through on\-demand counselling services and regular workplace clinics. You will be invited to celebrate community events such as sports days, fun\-runs, in\-house sports teams and beach clean ups. In addition to your competitive package and benefits you will have access to a suite of policies that include hybrid working arrangements, individual athletic sponsorship, study assistance sponsorship, employee referral rewards.
 



  





 Our newest opportunity is for a talented
 **Director \- Project Controls** 
 to join our PMCM Division based in
 **Dubai OR Abu Dhabi, UAE** 
 . This position is responsible for establishing, managing and continuously improving KEO's project controls framework across GCC\-based projects. This core\-office position ensures the effective planning, scheduling, resourcing, progress monitoring, reporting, and document control of multi\-disciplinary consultancy projects.
 



  





 The role provides strategic oversight and governance to ensure that projects are delivered on time, within approved schedules, and in alignment with contractual, corporate, and client requirements. The Director will act as a trusted advisor to project directors and senior leadership, supporting informed decision\-making through accurate data, forecasting, and performance analysis.
 



  





 We envisage success in this role to include;
 



  




* Establish and maintain corporate standards, procedures, and best practices for project planning and scheduling across all projects.
* Provide strategic oversight of project baseline schedules, ensuring alignment with contracts, scopes, and milestones.
* Review, validate, and approve project schedules, recovery plans, and revised programs.
* Ensure integration of schedules across disciplines, phases, and stakeholders.
* Monitor schedule performance, identify critical path risks, and drive mitigation strategies in collaboration with project teams.
* Oversee project performance measurement systems, including progress tracking, earned value management, and KPI reporting.
* Ensure timely and accurate preparation of executive dashboards, monthly reports, and schedule risk assessments.
* Analyze trends, variances, and forecast impacts on project timelines and advise leadership on corrective actions.
* Support senior management with data\-driven insights for strategic planning and decision\-making.
* Lead the development and management of resource planning and forecasting across all projects in GCC.
* Ensure optimal allocation of personnel in line with project requirements, skills, availability, and workload.
* Work closely with HR, Operations, and Project Directors to address resource gaps, mobilization planning, and recruitment forecasting.
* Monitor utilization rates and proactively manage capacity planning to support business objectives.
* Support succession planning and development of project controls talent within the organization.



  





 To be successful in this dynamic opportunity, we envision that your career journey to date will include minimum 15 years work experience partnered with a Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Constructuon Management , Project Management or related discipline.
 



  





 Chartered status or Professional Membership will be viewed favourably.
 



  





 KEO’s performance is founded upon integrity, results, innovation, safety and our people. We embrace diversity and recognise its’ contribution to client success wherever we work.
 



 We are excited to hear how you can partner with our award\-winning team and treat all expressions of interest confidentially.

data.title: Construction Director
data.raw_snippet:
The Construction Director is the senior leader responsible for overseeing the delivery performance of the EPC contractor across the full project life cycle. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role leads project planning, design coordination, procurement, construction, and commissioning.
   

  

 T
data.jd_text:
The Construction Director is the senior leader responsible for overseeing the delivery performance of the EPC contractor across the full project life cycle. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role leads project planning, design coordination, procurement, construction, and commissioning.
   

  

 The successful candidate will help ensure the project is delivered on time, within budget, to the required quality standards, and in line with safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements. This is a key leadership role for someone with strong experience in large and complex civil infrastructure projects.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

**Project Planning And Delivery**
* Develop, implement, and oversee the overall project plan, including schedules, budgets, resource planning, and quality control procedures.
* Make sure project delivery remains aligned with the company's strategic objectives.


**Construction Execution And Site Leadership**
* Lead and monitor all construction activities, including civil works, tunnelling, pipe\-laying, treatment plant construction, and related infrastructure.
* Support efficient site progress, compliance with specifications, and timely resolution of on\-site issues.


**Contract, Procurement, And Contractor Management**
* Oversee the selection, negotiation, and management of major EPC contractors, subcontractors, and key suppliers.
* Ensure contractors and suppliers meet their contractual obligations and perform effectively.


**Risk, Quality, And Compliance**
* Identify, assess, and manage project risks across technical, operational, financial, safety, and environmental areas.
* Implement strong quality assurance and quality control practices to support delivery of a high\-quality and sustainable asset.


**Stakeholder Management And Reporting**
* Act as the main point of contact for technical and construction\-related matters with the CEO, Board, lenders' technical advisors, regulatory authorities, and other key stakeholders.
* Provide clear, accurate, and regular progress updates to leadership and stakeholders.


 Academic Requirements
   

  

* Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a closely related field from a reputable university.
* Master's degree in Engineering, Project Management, or Business Administration (MBA) is highly preferred.
* Professional Engineer (PE) licence or an equivalent professional accreditation in civil engineering or construction.
* Project Management Professional (PMP) or an equivalent certification (for example PgMP or MSP) is highly desirable.
* Ongoing professional development in advanced construction techniques, tunnelling methods, or water/wastewater infrastructure is an advantage.


**Experience Requirements**
* Minimum of 20 years of progressive experience in civil infrastructure construction.
* At least 10 years in a senior project management or Construction Director role on mega\-projects valued above USD 2 billion.
* Proven success in leading the full life cycle delivery of at least one large\-scale tunnelling project and/or sewage or wastewater treatment plant project, from concept through commissioning.
* Extensive experience managing large, multidisciplinary construction teams, including direct labour, contractors, and consultants, in fast\-paced and high\-pressure environments.
* Strong understanding of project delivery methods such as EPC and Design\-Build, including management of complex interfaces between work packages.
* Demonstrated experience developing and managing project budgets above USD 1 billion, schedules, risk registers, and cost control and progress reporting systems.


 Key Skills and Competencies
   

  

* Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to motivate teams, resolve conflicts, and negotiate effectively with a wide range of stakeholders.
* Strong technical knowledge in tunnelling (including TBM operations and ground improvement), large\-diameter pipe\-laying, and advanced wastewater treatment processes.
* Ability to make important decisions under pressure, solve complex technical and logistical issues, and adapt to changing project conditions.
* High level of resilience and adaptability, with the ability to manage uncertainty and respond effectively during critical situations.
* Good knowledge of international construction standards, HSE regulations, and quality management systems such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 / OHSAS 18001\.
* Advanced proficiency in project management software such as Primavera P6, MS Project, BIM tools, and other relevant construction management technologies.

data.title: Director International Regulatory Policy and Intelligence (International Regulatory Affairs) – MEA & Eurasia
data.raw_snippet:
**JOB TITLE**
**Director International Regulatory Policy and Intelligence (International Regulatory Affairs) – MEA \& Eurasia**
**About Astrazeneca**
 AstraZeneca is a global, science\-led, patient\-focused biopharmaceutical company that focuses on the discovery, development, and commercialisation o
data.jd_text:
**JOB TITLE**
**Director International Regulatory Policy and Intelligence (International Regulatory Affairs) – MEA \& Eurasia**
**About Astrazeneca**
 AstraZeneca is a global, science\-led, patient\-focused biopharmaceutical company that focuses on the discovery, development, and commercialisation of prescription medicines for some of the world’s most serious diseases. But we’re more than one of the world’s leading pharmaceutical companies.
   

  

**Role Summary**
 This role is a strategic role with responsibility as international regulatory policy and intelligence director focusing on the MEA and Eurasia Region (EAEU, Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia) and working closely with Senior Area Regulatory Director (SARD)\- MEA, SARD Eurasia, International Policy \& Intelligence Senior Director, Marketing Companies within the region, and regional and global functions. This role will be part of International Regulatory Policy and Intelligence Team.
   

  

 The International Director for MEA and Eurasia Regulatory Policy and Intelligence is responsible for driving the development of impactful regulatory policy and advocacy with primary focus in the MEA and Eurasia regions and key international topics in cooperation with global, regional, local and functional experts. Also, will lead regulatory intelligence and legislation surveillance across the region, translating legislative developments and regulatory trends into strategic insights that enable to anticipate challenges, seize opportunities, and ensure our medicines reach patients across the region.
   

  

 The Director provides a broad strategic view for regulatory policy issues based on in\-depth knowledge of the internal and external regulatory environment in MEA and Eurasia and across key international topics .The Director will contribute to and co\-develop policy positions including stakeholder interactions and help to implement these policy positions regionally, leading to more impactful external engagements for AZ’s regulatory science priority topics, disease areas of interest, new therapeutic modalities, CMC \& quality, and cross\-area topics.
   

  

 The Director will be a major participant in articulating AZ’s consolidated view on key international and MEA and Eurasia Region regulatory guidance and regulations. This includes maintaining and developing key health authority relationships and being able to influence proposed rules and regulations that specifically impact AZ and ultimately the pharmaceutical industry. The incumbent can also lead implementation of changes to international strategy and processes due to significant regulatory environment changes (new legislation/guidance etc.) and can lead AZ’s response to key regulatory policy topics within MEA and Eurasia, and at Global and International Level. The director will assess the impact of new legislative/environmental changes on product strategy and communicate relevant information to international Regulatory Affairs (inRA) Strategy groups for onward communication to Global Project Teams
   

  

 The Director will have a major role interacting with other parts of the AZ organization that are influencing policy and legislation at local, regional, and global levels and developing advocacy plans for their areas of responsibility whilst capturing key KPIs and metrics.
   

  

 The Director attends relevant external meetings or conferences of value to the business, and contributes to the overall strategy, direction and efficient operation of the function. There will be an opportunity for the incumber to engage with Governments, trade associations, and other stakeholders on international policy issues and represent AZ in the relevant platforms externally.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Lead or manage regulatory policy priority topics as coordinated by the Global Regulatory Policy Team /International Policy and Intelligence lead
* Provide key regulatory procedural/ environmental advice on assigned topics to key customers/stakeholders within international and Global, including the Regulatory TAs, Commercial, MC regulatory, R\&D, Operations and CMO functions.
* Support preparation of regulatory policy documents, including position statements, reports, draft papers and presentation materials.
* Support with the oversight and management of international policy and intelligence scorecard KPIs, and its metrics for performance internally and externally
* Support the development for the International Regulatory Policy Training Capability Framework within MEA and Eurasia
* Establish the international CMC\-RA Policy Framework in collaboration with international CMC RA
* Represents AZ International Regulatory Affairs on selected external International Trade Association committees, with focus on MEA and Eurasia, to ensure AZ International Regulatory views on key issues are represented
* Attend relevant external meetings/conferences, with focus in MEA and Eurasia, to obtain relevant intelligence of value to the business and communicate effectively.
* May represent AZ in external benchmarking on advocacy business practices.
* Has personal responsibility for creating a culture of courageous leadership, creativity and collaboration
* Contribute to the strategy, direction and efficient operation of the immediate team and function.


**Essential For The Role**
* 10\+ years in global regulatory affairs, including international regions (Asia, Latin America and Middle East \& Africa)
* Understands the role of external policy and advocacy and connections to business goals in the International Regions.
* Demonstrated expertise in Regulatory policy related matters, through Health Authority or Industry experience, and more specifically in two or more International Regions such as Asia, Eurasia, Latin America and Middle East \& Africa.
* Working knowledge of AZ focused disease areas, new therapeutic modalities, CMC \& quality, and cross\-area topics.
* Thorough understanding and application of the Drug Development and associated regulatory processes globally and internationally. Prior experience with phase 3 design including end of phase 2 health authority interactions is desired. Contribution to a regulatory approval including leading response team and labelling negotiations would be a plus.
* Working experience in developing and executing new product strategies for global and international regions across different therapeutic areas
* A collaborative, entrepreneurial mindset—comfortable with ambiguity and adept at building from the ground up
* Demonstrated proficiencies in critical thinking, critical influencing, innovation, initiative, leadership and excellent oral and written communication skills.
* The ability to think strategically and critically and evaluate risks to regulatory activities.
* Collaborative and able to build consensus with peers and leadership (ability to influence upwards)
* Strong oral and written communication skills
* Experience in knowledge management and digital information management


**Desirable for the role**
* Experience working in Health Authority
* Problem solving skills
* Focus on delivery and results
* Experience in new modalities ie antibody conjugates, cell and gene therapy, radiotherapeutics
* Experience of participating in key industry groups and policy for especially within MEA and Eurasia
* Excellent strategic influencing and negotiation
* Experienced Regulatory Professional with degree in scientific discipline, typically pharmacy and biological science.
* Experience or aptitude in using AI/Gen AI within regulatory space.


**Why AstraZeneca?**
 At AstraZeneca we’re dedicated to being a Great Place to Work. Where you are empowered to push the boundaries of science and unleash your entrepreneurial spirit. There’s no better place to make a difference to medicine, patients, and society. An inclusive culture that champions diversity and collaboration, and always committed to lifelong learning, growth, and development. We’re on an exciting journey to pioneer the future of healthcare.
   

  

**So, what’s next?**
* Are you already imagining yourself joining our team? Good, because we can’t wait to hear from you.
* Are you ready to bring new ideas and fresh thinking to the table? Brilliant! We have one seat available, and we hope it’s yours.


**Where can I find out more?**
 Our Social Media, Follow AstraZeneca on LinkedIn
   

  

 Follow AstraZeneca on Facebook
   

  

 Follow AstraZeneca on Instagram
   

  

**Date Posted**
 20\-Apr\-2026
   

  

**Closing Date**
 02\-May\-2026
   

  

 AstraZeneca embraces diversity and equality of opportunity. We are committed to building an inclusive and diverse team representing all backgrounds, with as wide a range of perspectives as possible, and harnessing industry\-leading skills. We believe that the more inclusive we are, the better our work will be. We welcome and consider applications to join our team from all qualified candidates, regardless of their characteristics. We comply with all applicable laws and regulations on non\-discrimination in employment (and recruitment), as well as work authorization and employment eligibility verification requirements.

data.title: Senior Project Manager - Ports and Marine
data.raw_snippet: Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development
data.jd_text:
Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development. Operating in 100 countries, Egis puts the expertise of its 19,500 employees at the service of its clients and develops cutting\-edge innovations accessible to all projects. Through its wide range of activities, Egis is a key player in the collective organization of society and the living environment of citizens all over the world.
   

  





 With 3,500 employees across 8 countries in the Middle East, Egis has delivered over 700 complex development projects, stimulating economic growth and enhancing quality of life. Ranked among the top ten firms in the Middle East by Engineering News Record (ENR), Egis is committed to sustainable development. The Group’s operations in the Middle East are built on strategic acquisitions and a deep understanding of local market conditions. Egis’ long history of providing comprehensive engineering, consulting, and project management services makes it a trusted partner for regional governments, investors, and developers.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

 We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to lead complex port and marine infrastructure projects in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. In this pivotal role, you will oversee the planning, execution, and delivery of large\-scale maritime projects while managing cross\-functional teams, stakeholders, and resources. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong leadership capabilities, analytical thinking, and a comprehensive understanding of ports and marine operations in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment.
   

  




* Direct end\-to\-end project delivery for ports and marine infrastructure initiatives, ensuring adherence to scope, schedule, and budget parameters
* Develop and maintain detailed project plans, timelines, and resource allocation strategies using industry\-standard project management tools
* Identify, assess, and mitigate project risks through proactive analysis and contingency planning
* Manage stakeholder communications and expectations across multiple organizational levels and external partners
* Oversee contract negotiations, vendor management, and procurement activities in compliance with organizational policies
* Lead and mentor project teams, fostering collaboration and accountability to achieve project objectives
* Monitor project performance metrics and prepare comprehensive status reports for senior leadership
* Ensure compliance with maritime regulations, safety standards, and local regulatory requirements specific to UAE operations
* Coordinate with engineering, operations, and technical teams to resolve issues and optimize project outcomes
* Conduct post\-project reviews and implement lessons learned to enhance future project delivery


**Qualifications**

 \*\*Required Qualifications:\*\*
   

  




* Minimum 8\+ years of project management experience, with at least 5 years in ports, marine, or maritime infrastructure projects
* Professional project management certification (PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent)
* Demonstrated expertise in budget management, resource planning, and schedule optimization
* Strong knowledge of maritime industry operations, port facilities, and marine engineering principles
* Proficiency with project management software (MS Project, Primavera, or similar platforms)
* Excellent stakeholder management and interpersonal skills
* Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
* Proven ability to lead cross\-functional teams and manage complex stakeholder relationships



 \*\*Preferred Qualifications:\*\*
   

  




* Experience managing projects in the Middle East region
* Familiarity with marine terminal operations and cargo handling systems
* Experience with design\-build or engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) project delivery models
* Advanced certifications in risk management or specialized maritime project management


**Additional Information**

 Degree in Civil engineering

data.jd_text:
Currie \& Brown is looking for a
 **Project Director** 
 to join our project management team in Dubai, UAE working on
 **Data Centre** 
 projects.
 



  





**What will you be doing?** 




  




* Responsible for project definition and delivery
* Responsible for establishing project delivery plans and appropriate control mechanisms to assure effective delivery of the project
* Responsible for leading the project team and performance management of the Currie \& Brown team
* Work within established financial systems to independently produce plans and schedules and track and manage budgets, highlighting variances in order to manage and control project delivery targets
* Use own knowledge and experience of industry best practice to review and manage project processes and policies, providing insightful recommendations that support senior management to improve processes and systems
* Interact effectively and proactively across the organisation, seeking to develop ways to support collaboration and ensure projects can be delivered to meet customer expectations
* Advise senior management on progress and issues relating to own project, providing subject matter advice in order to support successful delivery in line with project objectives and strategies
* Present any business project risks or issues to senior managers (with proposed mitigation) to ensure the project is not adversely impacted, and ensure the delivery of projects to compliant standards
* Liaise and collaborate with clients and stakeholders, in order to ensure all parties are informed and aware of project progress and expectations
* Deliver aspects of Currie \& Brown’s vision to their respected project(s)



  





**What do you need?** 




  




* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent
* Experience of leading Data centre projects previously
* Project management professional certification or accreditation
* Proven team leader capabilities, behaviours and values
* PMC Experience
* Experience during both pre\-contract and post\-contract phases
* Knowledge of industry best practice
* Excellent contract administration experience
* Commercial acumen and the ability to manage relationships with external stakeholders
* Proven ability as a trusted advisor to the client



  





**Our corporate values** 




  





 At Currie \& Brown, we know that when it comes to a good idea, it doesn't matter where it comes from. That's why we take a collaborative, open\-door approach to running our business. Our people, from apprentices to senior management, make our company what it is. Everyone in the Currie \& Brown family, no matter how experienced, contributes to our success in their own way, and we have fun doing it.
 



  





 Unlike other companies, we focus on the long term and deliver on our commitments. We take a compassionate and inclusive approach to our work and always invest in the future of our people. Whether it's the desire to work internationally, the urge to develop, or even the curiosity to work in a new sector, we are there for our people at every stage, offering guidance, support and the structure to achieve. That's why so many of us have been building successful careers here for so long.
 



  





 With the backing of our parent company, we are an independent company. Our people are able to make quick and flexible decisions, and work directly with some of the world's best partner brands in our industry through Dar Group.
 



  





 This mix of our scale and size, our partnerships and our people makes us a true industry leader around the world.

data.title: Director Business Development - Lummus Consultants
data.raw_snippet:
**About the Company** 




  





  





 At Lummus Technology, innovation thrives, and excellence is a shared pursuit. When you join Lummus, you are empowered to make an impact and be part of a dynamic team that celebrates creativity, encourages professional development, and fosters collaboration
data.jd_text:
**About the Company** 




  





  





 At Lummus Technology, innovation thrives, and excellence is a shared pursuit. When you join Lummus, you are empowered to make an impact and be part of a dynamic team that celebrates creativity, encourages professional development, and fosters collaboration. From our technology portfolio to our global workforce, Lummus is growing at a rapid pace, and we hope that you will grow with us during this exciting time. Lummus Consultants International (LCI) is an autonomous business within Lummus Technology that provides technical, environmental \& social and management consulting services worldwide. We work primarily in the energy industry, with a depth of expertise in power generation, oil \& gas, refining and petrochemicals. We maintain a very strong presence in the international technical due diligence markets to support the global financial industry. LCI has a far\-reaching organization with offices in the United States, UK, the Middle East and Asia.
 



  





  





**About the Role** 




  





  





 Lummus Consultants is looking for a Business Development Director to grow the company into the area of technical and management consulting in the energy industry – offshore and onshore oil \& gas, LNG, pipelines, power generation and transmission, as well as refining and petrochemicals. This role will be expected to help grow the consulting practice area in the fields of oil \& Gas, power delivery and decarbonization and will primarily entail growing relationships with the financial community and project developers. Assignments require a high level of experience and ability to focus quickly on important issues. The individual we are seeking must be comfortable and confident to work in a seller/doer organization structure.
 



  





  





**Responsibilities** 




  





  




* Grow Lummus Consultants’ business – in cooperation with the existing business heads. His growth strategy should be heavily focused on technical solutions but with a broad business perspective beyond traditional due diligence assignments.
* Develop a business plan to capitalize on existing talents and resources within Lummus Consultants and Lummus Technology; the candidate’s strengths; and market opportunities.
* Grow name recognition of Lummus Consultants in all Energy industry segments (with assistance from the Lummus Communication team and other internal resources).
* Carry out business development, marketing, and proposal preparation efforts and lead Key Account Management teams efforts.
* Lead or participate in consulting project work.
* Prepare clear and concise written reports on assignments, develop conclusions and present clear and precise written and verbal results to clients.
* Expand the Lummus Consulting practice area (globally), consistent with the business plan – identify gaps in skills and potential recruits.
* Achieve objectives with minimal oversight.
* Management of the P\&L forecast by close collaboration with his supervisor and finance.
* Report to Senior Director of Lummus Consultants in Dubai office.



  









**Qualifications** 




  





  




* A degree\-educated Engineer; MBA is a plus but not required.
* The candidate should have at least 20 years relevant professional experience.
* Prior experience with an owner/operator or major engineering company and/or consultancy.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills, including ability to write clear and concise reports in English.
* The candidate should have strong interpersonal skills and be comfortable conversing with clients and senior management.
* Demonstrated management and project management skills.



  









**Required Skills** 




  





  




* Strong leadership abilities and excellent interpersonal skills.
* Strong communicator with excellent written and oral communication skills.
* Must have a high degree of self\-motivation and energy.
* Must have proven ability to manage business forecasting, short and long term projection.
* Capability of earning respect from the team and capable of influencing others without authority.
* Excellent professional image/presentation.
* Knowledge of many cultures/international style.
* Strong commercial savvy and negotiating skills.
* Interest in being knowledgeable in all services offerings in Lummus Consultants portfolio.
* Ability to work well with finance partners.
* Must have a strong balance of technical and commercial capabilities.



  





  





 Dubai office. Relocation is not being offered. Job\-related travel (business trips usually requiring less than a two\-week duration; often just one or two days) which includes international travel to financial institutions in London, is an important part of the work. Time away from the office may average 10 to 30 percent of work hours.
 



  





  





 Lummus Technology is the global leader in developing technology solutions that make modern life possible and focus on a more sustainable, low carbon future. We license process technologies in clean fuels, renewables, petrochemicals, polymers, gas processing and supply lifecycle services, catalysts, proprietary equipment and digitalization to customers worldwide.

data.title: Head of Accounts Payable Section
data.location: Sharjah, Sharjah Emirate, United Arab Emirates

We are passionate about building a collaborative and sustainable environment that cultivates twenty\-first\-century skills and f
data.jd_text:
The University of Sharjah is committed to providing a world\-class educational experience that prepares lifelong learners and leaders with integrated knowledge and skills. We are passionate about building a collaborative and sustainable environment that cultivates twenty\-first\-century skills and fosters pioneering research and scholarship. We seek to serve the current and future needs of our local community and beyond by offering innovative academic and professional programs.
 



 Since its foundation in 1997, the University has successfully become one of the top Universities in the UAE and has managed to make its mark in the educational and research industry worldwide united by six core values: Integrity and Ethics, Dedication, Inclusiveness, Excellence with limitless Drive, Accountability and Transparency, and Innovation and Creativity.
 



 The University of Sharjah believes in the crucial role of scientific research in the development of the modern United Arab Emirates. Since its establishment, the university has put research at the top of its list of priorities. Part of the university's strategy is to promote research among faculty members in all colleges, with the aim of attracting outstanding faculty with impressive research accomplishments.
 



  





**The University of Sharjah’s Rankings**




  





 251\-300 in Times Higher Education World University Rankings 2023
 



 Among the top 200 universities globally (176\-200\) in The World University Rankings 2023 by Subjects
 



 Among the best \#121 universities in Asia In News Best Global Universities Rankings 2022/2023
 



 \=89th in The Young University Rankings in 2022
 



 \#61 The Emerging Economies University Rankings in 2022
 



 Among the top 10 best Arab universities The Arab University Rankings 2022
 



  





**About the Role:**




  





 As the Head of Accounts Payable Section and a member of the
 **Finance Department** 
 team, you are expected to ensure that the department operates smoothly, maintain accurate and complete payment records related to suppliers, lenders, and employees in a timely and efficient manner, and process payments from clients as per the University of Sharjah's policies and procedures. As well as manage, develop and motivate the section's team members.
 



  





**Key Duties \& Responsibilities:**




  





 1\. Monitor day\-to\-day Accounts Payable activities, and ensure that business processes and workflows are executed correctly and efficiently. As well as ensure that job tasks are distributed between team members fairly and professionally.
 



 2\. Lead the development and execution of the Accounts Payable section's processes and procedures, and ensure that they are in alignment with best practices and UOS's requirements.
 



 3\. Manage the accounts payable process, ensuring that all payments related to external clients, suppliers, and vendors are settled in a timely and efficient manner.
 



 4\. Ensure compliance with all contractual payment policy terms and conditions, and report any matters of concern to the line manager for immediate action.
 



 5\. Maintain and control all accounts payable data to meet the requirements of internal and external auditors, and ensure the accuracy of financial statements to ensure conformity of information to acceptable standards.
 



 6\. Supervise invoice transactions including receipt of invoices, verification, record of transaction, and direct expense. As well as follow up where the authorization to pay has not been provided in order to ensure that payments are completed in a timely manner.
 



 7\. Examine all vouchers posted and address any issues related to the accounts payable process to ensure their resolution, as well as prepare any related documentation and clarifications required by the Compliance and Internal Audit Office
 



 8\. Ensure that vendor invoices are properly approved and in line with the official contracts, resolve all payments or billing issues with vendors, as well as review and post vendor's payments in the Banner System.
 



 9\. Provide leadership to the section's team in order to build and develop their organizational capabilities, provide them with development opportunities, as well as report on their performance.
 



 10\. Participate in the hiring process for the section including identifying job requirements, sorting and reviewing candidate resumes, and contributing to the selection and interview process in order to ensure the best and most suitable candidates are selected for the available vacancies.
 



 11\. Support the director in the development of the Finance department's KPIs by providing professional input and advice on developing and achieving the relevant KPIs. As well as report on the section's progress, and prepare the necessary action plans to the director. In addition to ensuring that each team member has a good understanding of the section's KPIs and how their roles impact their achievement.
 



 12\. Assist the director in the Finance Department's projects that help support and improve the department and the university in general, and supervise the project team when needed to ensure each member completes their work tasks accurately and on time.
 



 13\. Participate in the preparation of the department's annual budget, in collaboration with the director and other heads, and track the section's expenditures and ensure they are within the limits of the budget.
 



 14\. Prepare various reports, analyses and statistics related to the Accounts Payable section when requested by the line manager.
 



 15\. Complete any other job\-related tasks as assigned by the line manager within the expected time limit.
 



  





**Job Requirements:**




 ▪ Minimum of bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or a relevant discipline.
 



 ▪ A professional qualification as a chartered accountant or qualified member of an accountancy body (e.g. CIMA, ACCA, ICAS, etc.)
 



 ▪ 12\-14 years of UAE experience in a similar role preferably in higher education institutions.
 



  





 ▪ Excellent oral and written communication skills and the ability to prepare professional documents, in both Arabic and English.
 



 ▪ Financial accounting and management skills.
 



 ▪ Ability to work collaboratively across departmental functions.
 



 ▪ Demonstrate a good understanding of operating the required ERP system/software (e.g. Banner).
 



 ▪ Excellent leadership skills.
 



 ▪ Time management skills
 



 ▪ Analytical and critical thinking.
 



 ▪ Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving skills.
 



 ▪ Clear presentation skills and ability to influence.
 



 ▪ Experience in managing teams including the development and delivery of on\-the\-job training and mentoring.
 



 ▪ Ability to work with diverse business units and to quickly adapt and understand key business challenges.
 



  





 Closing Date: 16/May/2026

data.jd_text:
**ABOUT US** 




 YKONE is a global marketing group trusted by iconic brands from the fashion, luxury, travel \& beauty industries. Since 2008, we have been helping brands to connect with, engage or grow their audience through impactful stories crafted and spread by the world’s most powerful or creative influencers.
 



 Our creative DNA \& data\-driven approach allows us to conduct international projects for various actors including Nespresso, L’Oréal, Dior, Chanel, TAG Heuer, Estée Lauder as well as several Tourism Offices in the world (Visit Abu Dhabi, Visit Turkey, Visit the USA…).
 



 Headquartered in Paris, YKONE has offices in Milan, Dubai, Berlin, Munich, Abu Dhabi, Geneva, Miami, Riyadh, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Doha and Las Vegas.
 



 The International Assistant Project Manager will report to the International Client Director and join the International Project Management team.
 



  





**The role** 




 We are looking for a Client Director to lead Ykone’s most strategic client relationships and drive the agency’s commercial growth. This is a senior leadership role, acting as a key partner to the general manager, a bridge between clients, strategy, and financial performance. You will bring strong financial acumen, market intelligence, and executive presence, ensuring that every engagement delivers both creative excellence and business impact.
 



  





**Key responsibilities** 



* Lead and own relationships with top\-tier clients at a C\-suite level
* Act as a strategic advisor, shaping long\-term partnerships and growth plans
* Drive high\-level conversations around brand, business, and market opportunities
* Own and drive revenue growth, profitability, and financial performance across accounts
* Develop and oversee budgets, forecasts, and margin optimization
* Lead pricing strategies, contract negotiations, and commercial structuring
* Build and manage a high\-value pipeline of opportunities
* Identify and unlock new revenue streams across clients and markets
* Oversee proposal development balancing creative ambition and commercial viability
* Ensure strong cross\-functional alignment with creative, strategy, and production teams



  





**What We’re Looking For** 



* 12\+ years in senior client leadership, commercial, or business roles
* Strong financial acumen (P\&L ownership, margins, forecasting, pricing)
* Proven track record managing high\-value clients and driving revenue growth
* Experience in complex deal structuring and negotiations
* Strong understanding of luxury, culture, retail, and experiential industries
* Executive presence with ability to present and influence at C\-level
* Strategic thinking combined with strong commercial instinct
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management
* Full\-time, on\-site role based in Dubai



  





**Mindset** 



* A business\-first leader with a strong understanding of numbers and growth
* A trusted advisor capable of influencing senior stakeholders
* Highly ownership\-driven with a focus on results and impact
* Strong judgment, presence, and credibility
* Thrives in a fast\-moving, entrepreneurial environment



  





**How to Apply** 




 Please submit your CV along with any relevant portfolio, case studies, or business
