data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
   

  

 At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
   

  

**Job Description**
 SH\&E MANAGER
   

  

**Position Overview**
 Responsibility for Safety Management in the Cliff Stabilization project for SAPL Qiddiya Infrastructure Partner (QIDP). Responsible and accountable for ensuring that all work is in full coordination with Qiddiya Investment Company (QIC). QIC is providing strategic direction on a city\-wide basis. Reports directly to the Project Director.
   

  

 Under general oversight of a safety, health, and environmental (SH\&E) director, Project Director, provides expertise, advice, and technical assistance to one or more project or program managers in implementing company risk management practices and in complying with the prevailing regulatory and client SH\&E requirements. Represents the company in all SH\&E matters pertaining to internal and external activities as associated with assigned projects and programs. Reports by dotted line to one or more project or program managers and reports directly to the Parsons corporate SH\&E functional group through the SH\&E director.
   

  

 Specific Responsibilities
   

  

* Leads full\-time, part\-time, and collateral duty project and program SH\&E specialists in implementing work plans and the company’s risk management practices.
* Represents the business unit SH\&E director on project or program worksites.
* Advises and assists project manager, program manager and/or other project and program personnel in conforming to Parsons’ Environmental, Safety, Health, and Risk Program (ESHARP) management system and Parsons’ policies and procedures.
* Develops site\-specific work plans, SH\&E compliance programs, training programs, and orientation plans fully conforming to relevant SH\&E regulations, Parsons’ ESHARP management system, and client requirements.
* Collaborates with project and program managers to evaluate (sub)contractors’ SH\&E performance, planning, and culture to be consistent with the company’s risk management practices and core values.
* Audits projects and programs and their worksites against corporate standards; creates and submits written audit reports.
* Inspects worksites to detect hazards and provides expertise to project personnel to eliminate/control the SH\&E risks associated with exposure to the hazards; creates and submits written inspection reports.
* Implements Parsons’ occupational injury and incident reporting and management processes.
* Initiates and conducts thorough incident investigations and root cause analyses using defensible protocols.
* Collaborates with project or program managers, craft workers, and other project or program personnel to identify effective corrective actions; creates and submits comprehensive investigation reports and lessons learned.
* Performs trends analyses on audit findings, inspection findings, and incident reports; creates and submits regular analysis reports.
* Represents Parsons during external regulatory or loss control inspections of project or program worksites.
* Assists with risk assessments for upcoming work and provides SH\&E subject matter expertise to work planning processes.
* Seeks active and personal participation from the project or program manager, craft workers, and other project or program personnel to identify SH\&E improvements and to implement the improvements.
* Assists the project or program manager with new hire orientation.
* Performs regulatory and best practice SH\&E training.
* Creates and maintains SH\&E training records.
* Participates in routine safe work briefings with craft, office, and (sub)contractor workers.
* Performs other responsibilities associated with this position as may be appropriate.


 Skills and Competencies
   

  

* Extensive knowledge of Federal and State health and safety regulations and reporting procedures
* Advanced knowledge of safety\-related national consensus standards (e.g., ANSI, NFPA)
* Exceptional interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills
* Ability to perform in a supervisory/managerial capacity


 Education and Typical Experience
   

  

* Bachelor's Degree in Occupational Safety and Health or related field (or equivalent experience)
* 10\+ years of related work experience in the field of Health and Safety, including some supervisory experience


 Licenses and Certifications
   

  

* Certified Safety Professional (CSP) or Certified Industrial Hygienist (CIH) is preferred


**Key Relationships**
* Supervision Received \- SH\&E Director; Project/Program Manager (various levels)
* Supervision Given \- SH\&E Specialists
* Internal Contacts \- All employees on construction projects
* External Contacts \- Suppliers; Clients; Safety Organizations; Government Organizations


 Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
   

  

 We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
   

  

 Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent\-recruitment/.

data.jd_text:
**Requisition ID:
 **292114****
* Relocation Authorized: International \- Single
* Telework Type: Full\-Time Office/Project
* Work Location: Riyadh


**Extraordinary Teams Building Inspiring Projects**
 Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown economies, improved the resiliency of the world's infrastructure, increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the world a safer, cleaner place.
   

  

 Differentiated by the quality of our people and our relentless drive to deliver the most successful outcomes, we align our capabilities to our customers' objectives to create a lasting positive impact. We serve the Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security \& Environmental; Energy; Mining \& Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span from initial planning and investment, through start\-up and operations.
   

  

 Core to Bechtel is our Vision, Values and Commitments . They are what we believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver. Learn more about our extraordinary teams building inspiring projects in our Impact Report .
   

  

**Project Overview**
 Expo 2030 Riyadh is a global platform where countries, organizations, and individuals will come together to collaborate. Under the theme “Foresight for Tomorrow,” the Expo promotes sustainable development, innovation, and transformative thinking. The event will be held from October 2030 to March 2031 and is expected to welcome over 40 million visits.
   

  

 The 6 km² Masterplan outlines a comprehensive framework for participation and infrastructure at the Expo. The exhibition space, totaling 491,100 m² GFA, can accommodate up to 197 participating countries and 29 international organizations. Working collaboratively with our customer, Expo 2030 Riyadh Company (ERC), Bechtel will manage the infrastructure program which comprises early works, utilities, and publicly accessible spaces like streets and parks. Our scope on the six\-square\-kilometer site will transition into a mixed\-use legacy development to serve the community after the six\-month event is over. The project’s master plan leverages international sustainability standards for urban afforestation, treated water, and energy sources.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The ideal candidate will have signiﬁcant leadership experience and a clear understanding of project management within KSA. He/she will have previous Project Controls experience of large scale (\>$1Bn) projects, ideally within KSA or the wider Middle East. He/she will have a cultural awareness of the nuances of operating successfully. within KSA and have proven success in managing client relationships at a senior level. Job Dimensions:
   

  

* Supervision Received Reports to and receives technical guidance and operational supervision from designated supervisory personnel. B. Supervision Exercised Provides technical and operational direction to assigned personnel. C. Contacts Interacts and partners with subordinate supervisors, customers, and/or functional peer group managers, involving matters between functional areas, customer and the company May lead a cooperative effort among members of a project team.


**Major Responsibilities**
* Establishes operational objectives and work plans, and delegates assignments to subordinates
* Provides structured leadership and guidance for the overall execution of Project Controls activities
* Manages the development of Project Controls procedures and processes that are aligned with customer requirements
* Manages cost engineering, cost estimating, cost analysis/control, planning/scheduling, risk management, and project reporting activities for the project, and staff planning activities that requires a high degree of technical skill and experience
* Manages cost estimating, analysis, and control activities
* Manages schedule development, maintenance, monitoring, impact identification, and recovery plan development activities
* Develops the preparation and presentation of project estimates and cost forecasts
* Supports project organizational and administrative activities
* Provides generally non\-routine cost engineering/planning and scheduling guidance
* Identifies and provides innovative solutions to cost engineering and planning/scheduling problems
* Reviews contract drafts for adequacy of cost and scope definition and Project Controls requirements
* Reviews Earned Revenue Determination Data, Financial Summaries, Project Financial Status Reports, and Charge for Project Working Capital calculations for the project
* Manages the coordination of project reports and dashboards as necessary
* Performs routine coaching, tasking and performance management of assigned Project Controls staﬀ and such additional professional tasks as are directed by the Project Manager


**Education And Experience Requirements**
* Requires bachelor's or master's degree (or international equivalent) plus a minimum of 15 years of professional experience or, in lieu of a degree, 21 or more years of professional experience


**Required Knowledge And Skills**
* Previous Project Controls project experience within KSA or the wider Middle East and across a broad range of infrastructure projects.
* Bachelor’s degree plus a minimum of 15 years of professional experience or, in lieu of a degree, 21 or more years of professional experience.
* Demonstrate experience and skill in directing cost engineering functions, including cost estimating, cost control, and cost analysis.
* Demonstrate experience and skill in directing, planning, and scheduling functions, including schedule development, control, and analysis.
* Have expertise in the delivery, communication, and training of risk management.
* Demonstrate experience and skill in establishing criteria for Project Controls activities.
* Show proven leadership skills in personnel administration, and in the training of technical and nontechnical personnel.
* Expert in oral and written communication, with demonstrated ability to present project information to Bechtel and client management.
* Signiﬁcant experience and a cultural understanding of project management.
* Previous experience of PFSR preparation, coordination and delivery.
* Advanced level of knowledge of engineering, procurement, contracts, construction, and startup work processes.
* Knowledge of engineering and construction management customarily acquired over time through specialized instruction or practical experience.
* Working knowledge of change management.
* Experience of knowledge transfer and the development of local employees.


**Total Rewards/Benefits**
 For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world's toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people thrive. Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total Rewards
   

  

**Diverse Teams Build The Extraordinary**
 As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This diversity has made us a more trusted partner, more effective problem solvers and innovators, and a more attractive destination for leading talent.
   

  

 We are committed to being a company where every colleague feels that they belong\-where colleagues feel part of "One Team," respected and rewarded for what they bring, supported in pursuing their goals, invested in our values and purpose, and treated equitably. Click here to learn more about the people who power our legacy.
   

  

**Bechtel is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, age, national origin, disability, citizenship status (except as authorized by law), protected veteran status, genetic information, and any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law. Applicants with a disability, who require a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application or hiring process, may e\-mail their request to** 
**acesstmt@bechtel.com**

data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?**
 At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.
   

  

 We are seeking Project/Development Management professionals for a project based in Riyadh to support the Planning \& Oversight department. This role will provide direct support by contributing hands‑on project delivery capability across multiple initiatives. Individuals should have strong experience working on projects of varying scales from inception through completion, with the ability to navigate project processes, documentation, and coordination requirements confidently.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Assist in preparation of project development strategy including project purpose \& objectives, project implementation, project design \& delivery plan, procurement, schedule and budget.
* Assist the Development director in Preparation reports and presentations as required.
* Support the project planning, coordination, budgeting and cashflow.
* Interpret project scope and constrains, plan the overall workflow stages and breakdown and provide timeline schedules accordingly.
* Support to establish and challenge the project Cost Estimates in accordance with the requirement for the development stage of the project.
* Monitor budgets, forecast and cost plans throughout the project lifecycle.
* Communicate and coordinate with cross functional the status and updates of stakeholders to inform the decisions.
* Coordinate and manage as required the internal and external stakeholders.
* Identify, assess, track and report on project risks and establish and implement mitigation strategies.
* Ensure the project procurement requirements are established and adhered to allowing the project to progress in accordance with the project implementation timeline. Participate in procurement activities as required.
* Support commercial processes as required.
* Monitor and control the consultants and contractor performance and progress and conduct coordination meetings with the related team for problem resolutions.
* Maintain accurate project documentation in accordance with company requirements including confidentiality. Collate project data and issue as required.
* Conduct a comprehensive benchmark analysis of comparable project locally and internationally
* Provide recommendation on product differentiation, quality standard and benchmark driven project features
* Maintain an updated benchmark database to support investment decision and project approvals
* Ensure all project activities adhere to company’s governance, environmental and safety standard
* Foster a collaborative, innovative, and result\-oriented team culture.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering /Architecture.
* 8\-12 Years of experience in Development / Project Management role.
* Experience in delivering projects KSA.
* Experience in projects from concept design through to execution.
* Worked client side through past experience.
* Proven ability to manage multiple large projects simultaneously.
* Proven ability to manage multiple internal and external stakeholders
* Strong organizational, presentation and communication skills.
* Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint and other relevant software.
* PMP or similar Certification is an advantage.
* Commercial acumen and negotiation skills
* Problem solving, risk management and supporting decision making


**Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.**
 Join our close\-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world.
   

  

**With us, you can. Apply today.**

data.title: Director Transaction Advisory
data.raw_snippet:
**Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional**
 From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your B
data.jd_text:
**Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional**
 From the projects you will work on to the clients we partner with. From career‑defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where everyone contributes to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it will be anything but ordinary.
   

  

**Here at Buro Happold, expect the exceptional.**
 From the projects you will work on to the clients we work with. From career\-defining opportunities to an inclusive and diverse practice where we all contribute to a more equitable world. From our reputation to our relationships. Wherever your Buro Happold career takes you, it’ll be anything but ordinary.
   

  

**Your next role**
 Buro Happold is supporting the transformation of cities and public services across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the wider GCC, aligned with Vision 2030, national transformation programmes, and public‑sector reform agendas. As Director – Transaction Advisory within our Municipal Services team, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping, structuring, and delivering complex public‑sector transactions across the region.
   

  

 Based in KSA, you will lead Transaction Advisory services across priority municipal sectors including urban services, mobility, utilities, public realm, mixed\-use development, sport and social infrastructure. You will work closely with senior public‑sector stakeholders and delivery entities.
   

  

 This role offers a rare opportunity to combine deep KSA market understanding, strong knowledge of the local NCP PPP lifecycle framework, and global best practice to help public authorities structure bankable, resilient, and outcome‑driven transactions that deliver long‑term value for citizens.
   

  

**Your key duties**
 As a Director, you will provide senior leadership to our Transaction Advisory and PPP offering within Municipal Services, driving growth, credibility, and excellence in delivery across KSA and the GCC.
   

  

 You will:
   

  

* Lead the Transaction Advisory strategy in KSA and the GCC for public‑sector projects, aligned with Buro Happold’s regional growth.
* Originate and lead PPP, privatization, and transaction advisory engagements, including project screening, feasibility studies, business cases, value‑for‑money assessments, and procurement strategies.
* Advise public‑sector clients across the full PPP lifecycle, including project identification, structuring, market sounding, procurement, bid evaluation, and support through financial close.
* Act as a trusted advisor to senior government, municipal, and delivery authority leadership, supporting decisions on service delivery models, private‑sector participation, and long‑term operating arrangements.
* Provide expert advice on commercial, contractual, and risk‑allocation structures, ensuring transactions are bankable, compliant with KSA frameworks, and aligned with policy and affordability objectives.
* Work closely with multidisciplinary teams (engineering, planning, operations, digital, economics) to deliver integrated, technically robust, and commercially sound transaction solutions.
* Lead high‑level client engagements, workshops, negotiations, and market‑facing interactions with government entities, investors, developers, operators, and lenders.
* Drive business development across KSA and the GCC, including strategic client relationships, opportunity origination, bid leadership, and framework participation.
* Represent Buro Happold with NCP, municipalities, ministries, and key industry stakeholders, strengthening our position as a trusted advisor in PPP and public‑sector transactions.
* Mentor and develop regional advisory talent, building a strong, locally grounded Transaction Advisory capability within the Municipal Services team.
* Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring quality assurance, commercial success, and effective risk management across advisory commissions.


**Your Skills And Experience**
 You will bring a combination of regional credibility, transaction expertise, and senior‑level leadership experience.
   

  

 You will have:
   

  

* A Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related discipline; a postgraduate qualification is highly desirable.
* Significant experience in Transaction Advisory, PPP, or Commercial Advisory, gained within a leading consultancy, advisory firm, or financial institution.
* Strong, demonstrable experience in the KSA market, including a clear understanding of government structures, procurement regulations, and the NCP PPP process/Framework.
* A proven track record delivering PPP, privatization, or investment transactions across municipal, infrastructure, or public‑service sectors in KSA and/or the GCC.
* Deep understanding of project delivery models, operating models, funding and financing structures, and risk allocation in public‑sector transactions.
* Established relationships with government entities, NCP, municipalities, investors, developers, and operators in the region.
* Strong leadership and people‑development skills, with experience building and leading senior advisory teams.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder‑management skills, with confidence operating at ministerial, executive, and board level.
* High analytical rigour, commercial judgement, and the ability to operate effectively in complex, multi‑stakeholder environments.


**What We Offer You**
 Bring your knowledge and expertise to one of the world’s most respected consultancies.
   

  

* Hybrid working arrangements
* Attractive salary and benefits package
* A strong commitment to your continuous development. Whatever stage in your career, we have a learning and development programme that will enable and empower you
* Entitlement to two memberships to professional bodies or subscriptions
* An international culture that wherever your career takes you at Buro Happold, you won’t be far from the exceptional.


**At Buro Happold, everyone else’s extraordinary is just our everyday.**
**Apply today and be a part of our legacy.**
**A place for everyone**
 Buro Happold values individual's flexible approaches to working patterns as an important part of the way you work and achieve balance. We welcome applications from those who are seeking flexibility in their careers.
   

  

 Our exceptional portfolio of projects is the result of the diversity of thought, identities, backgrounds and experiences that shape us. Appreciating each other’s differences is key, we want employees to feel they don’t have to mask elements of their identity to thrive.
   

  

 We are working to change the lack of representation of marginalised groups in the built environment and are particularly keen to hear from anyone who feels they are underrepresented in the industry. We have much to learn from one another. Embracing difference allows us to develop the most innovative and elegant solutions.
   

  

 Please let us know if there are any adjustments, we could make to the application process to make it easier and more comfortable for you. Contact recruitment@burohappold.com so we can work with you to support you throughout your application.

**Company Description**
 Saatchi \& Saatchi is a leading global communications and advertising network known for its impactful creative ideas that generate powerful emotional connections between consumers and brands. Present in over 67 countries, Saatchi \& Saatchi employs 6,500 professionals over 1
data.jd_text:
**Company Description**
 Saatchi \& Saatchi is a leading global communications and advertising network known for its impactful creative ideas that generate powerful emotional connections between consumers and brands. Present in over 67 countries, Saatchi \& Saatchi employs 6,500 professionals over 114 offices. Global clients include HSBC, P\&G, Toyota, Visa and GSK. Saatchi \& Saatchi is part of Publicis Communications a key division of Publicis Groupe, the world’s third largest communications group.
   

  

**Overview**
 The Content Manager will be responsible for creating, managing and executing social media content strategies for our clients in a digital, social media \& creative scope. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of social media platforms and the latest social media trends, as well as excellent copywriting skills.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Technical \& Product**
* Develop and implement social media content strategies and campaigns for clients.
* Expert writing and editing skills for various platforms and formats
* EN and AR Translation skills and good knowledge and command of both
* Knowledge in storytelling and communication narratives
* Content and publishing calendar creation
* Write and edit high\-quality social media content, including posts, captions, and blogs.
* Collaborate with creative teams and account managers to ensure brand consistency and client satisfaction.
* Stay up to date with the latest social media trends and best practices.
* Monitor industry social media activity


**Business Acumen \& Operational Efficiency**
* Strong business acumen with a demonstrated ability to understand client needs and objectives
* Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while ensuring quality and timeliness of deliverables
* Proven track record of meeting or exceeding project goals and targets
* Strong organizational and project management skills


**Innovative \& Analytical Mindset**
* Creative and innovative approach to social media content creation
* Ability to analyze data and metrics to inform content strategy
* Ability to think critically and solve problems


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Journalism, or a related field
* Minimum of 5\+ years of experience in social media content creation and management, preferably in an agency setting
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills


**Additional Information**
 R\-3749P\-4257

It seeks to lead the aviation industry by transforming Saudi Arabia into a global aviation and trade hub \- a digitally native airline that will connect the kingdom to more than 100
data.jd_text:
Riyadh Air (RX), headquartered in the Saudi Capital, is the new national airline that’s shaping the future of flying. It seeks to lead the aviation industry by transforming Saudi Arabia into a global aviation and trade hub \- a digitally native airline that will connect the kingdom to more than 100 destinations.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Are you a technology delivery leader who thrives on complexity, scale, and the challenge of connecting enterprise systems in a fast moving, digital first environment?
   

  

 In this pivotal role, we are seeking a Manager, Integration Delivery to lead the end to end delivery of our integration capabilities.
   

  

 You'll will shape and execute the enterprise integration delivery model, ensuring secure, reliable, and scalable connectivity across internal systems and third party platforms that underpin the airline’s digital and operational ecosystem.
   

  

 Importantly, you are required to drive integration delivery excellence by analysing current integration maturity, defining target state architectures, and managing complex, multi stream delivery across APIs, messaging, and event driven integrations.
   

  

 Central to your success in this role, you will work closely with Product, Architecture, Engineering, DevOps, and business stakeholders to ensure predictable delivery, strong governance, and alignment with enterprise standards and business priorities.
   

  

 This is a unique opportunity to influence Riyadh Air’s core technology foundation and play a critical role in building one of the aviation industry’s most advanced digital integration landscapes.
   

  

**About You**
 Degree qualified, you will demonstrate a minimum of 10 years of IT delivery experience with a strong focus on enterprise integration initiatives, integration platforms, or complex technology programs.
   

  

 You'll bring hands‑on experience delivering integration solutions using modern integration patterns such as API‑led connectivity, messaging, and event‑driven architectures, along with a strong track record of leading cross‑functional teams and working with engineering, architecture, and product partners to deliver complex, platform‑level capabilities.
   

  

 Critically, you should have strong analytical instincts and the ability to translate business, architectural, and technical requirements into clear delivery plans, roadmaps, and execution strategies.
   

  

 Your ability to manage complex stakeholders, govern delivery execution, mitigate risk, and drive continuous improvement across integration delivery will make you successful in this role.
   

  

 We’d love to hear from you even more if you have experience within airline, travel, or large‑scale enterprise environments, familiarity with platforms such as IBM Cloud Pak for Integration or similar technologies, and experience operating within agile or product‑led delivery models.
   

  

 This is your opportunity to shape the future of air travel. Bring your best and show us why you can own this position within Riyadh Air.
   

  

 Please submit your application by clicking on the Apply button. Our Talent Acquisition team will be reviewing applications shortly.

data.title: Construction Director (Civil Infrastructure)
data.raw_snippet:
JASARA PMC is seeking a seasoned
 **Construction Director (Civil Infrastructure)** 
 to lead and manage the successful delivery of our civil infrastructure projects. This role is key to ensuring that large\-scale infrastructure projects are executed efficiently, safely, and in accordance with qualit
data.jd_text:
JASARA PMC is seeking a seasoned
 **Construction Director (Civil Infrastructure)** 
 to lead and manage the successful delivery of our civil infrastructure projects. This role is key to ensuring that large\-scale infrastructure projects are executed efficiently, safely, and in accordance with quality standards and client expectations.
   

  

 The Construction Director will coordinate with multidisciplinary teams, oversee project planning and execution, and ensure adherence to budgets, timelines, and regulatory requirements. This individual will act as a strategic leader driving operational excellence and innovation within civil infrastructure development.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Lead the planning, coordination, and oversight of civil infrastructure construction projects
* Manage multidisciplinary project teams to ensure effective collaboration and delivery of project objectives
* Ensure projects comply with all safety, quality, environmental, and regulatory standards
* Develop and control project budgets, schedules, and resource allocation
* Identify risks and implement mitigation strategies to minimize project impact
* Maintain strong client relationships and communicate project progress and challenges transparently
* Direct contract management, procurement processes, and subcontractor coordination
* Promote a culture of continuous improvement, safety awareness, and innovation
* Provide mentorship and leadership to project teams and junior staff


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field; advanced degree preferred
* At least 20 years of progressive experience in civil infrastructure construction management
* Strong background in civil infrastructure including sewer networks, roads and small to mid\-scale bridges/culverts (local traffic and pedestrian use)
* Strong leadership and team management skills with a focus on achieving project goals
* In\-depth knowledge of construction standards, regulations, and safety protocols
* Excellent communication, negotiation and stakeholder management abilities
* Experience in contract administration, procurement and budget control
* Ability to identify project risks and develop effective mitigation plans
* Proficiency in project management software and tools

data.title: Delivery Director - Commercial Office (CO106)
data.raw_snippet:
Qiddiya Investment Company is seeking a seasoned Delivery Director for our Commercial Office developments. This strategic role will lead the successful delivery of commercial office projects that meet Qiddiya's high standards for quality, functionality, and innovation.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
data.jd_text:
Qiddiya Investment Company is seeking a seasoned Delivery Director for our Commercial Office developments. This strategic role will lead the successful delivery of commercial office projects that meet Qiddiya's high standards for quality, functionality, and innovation.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Lead and oversee the end\-to\-end delivery of commercial office projects, ensuring adherence to timelines, budgets, and quality standards
* Collaborate with design, construction, engineering, and procurement teams to develop and execute effective delivery plans
* Manage stakeholder relationships, including contractors, consultants, regulatory authorities, and internal teams, ensuring clear communication and alignment across all parties
* Monitor project progress through regular site visits, reporting, and risk management to ensure successful project outcomes
* Implement best practices in project management, ensuring compliance with legal, safety, and quality regulations
* Provide senior management with timely and accurate project status updates, highlighting key risks and mitigation strategies
* Drive innovation and sustainability initiatives within projects to support Qiddiya's vision of creating a world\-class community


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Architecture, or a related field (Master's degree preferred)
* Minimum of 12 years of experience in delivering commercial office or similar large\-scale projects
* Proven leadership experience managing multidisciplinary teams and complex projects
* Strong knowledge of construction methodologies, project management processes, and regulatory requirements
* Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills
* Ability to analyze risks and implement effective mitigation strategies
* Proficiency in project management software and tools


**Benefits**
* The opportunity to be part of one of Saudi Arabia's most ambitious city developments
* A role dedicated to a specific residential asset type, allowing you to build deep expertise in your area
* A fast\-paced, high\-impact environment where strategic thinking and execution excellence are highly valued
* Collaboration with global industry leaders in real estate development, architecture, and planning

data.title: Nuclear Engineering Senior Expert
data.raw_snippet: At Amentum UK and International, we are at the forefront of shaping the future, not just solving problems. Our expertise in engineering, project management, and solutions integration has positioned us as a key collaborator with government entities, delivering intricate and impactful programs around
data.jd_text:
At Amentum UK and International, we are at the forefront of shaping the future, not just solving problems. Our expertise in engineering, project management, and solutions integration has positioned us as a key collaborator with government entities, delivering intricate and impactful programs around the world. With a strong presence in Saudi Arabia, we are actively involved in critical projects throughout the region. We are spearheading several exciting initiatives, taking on technical responsibilities for programme management, engineering oversight, and construction assurance.
   

  

 People are our greatest asset, and we offer a competitive package to retain and attract the best talent. In addition to the benefits you would expect, UK employees also receive free single medical cover and digital GP service, family\-friendly benefits such as enhanced parental leave pay and free membership of employee assistance and parental programmes, plus reimbursement towards relevant professional development and memberships. We also give back to our communities through our programmes which incorporate matched\-funding, paid volunteering time and charitable donations.
   

  

**About The Opportunity:**
 The Nuclear Engineering Director will report to the Chief Technical Officer (CTO) and will lead the Owner’s engineering oversight for a new nuclear power plant project during pre\-EPC Contract phase and EPC Contractor delivery phase, acting as the focal point for all technical deliverables and interfaces.
   

  

 This role ensures that the Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) Contractor’s engineering outputs meet project requirements, nuclear safety standards, and regulatory expectations throughout the project lifecycle. The Director coordinates closely with Owner licensing function to align design deliverables with License Applications, and manages all technical interfaces between the Owner, EPC contractor, and external infrastructure.
   

  

 A key part of the role is supporting the technical evaluation of the EPC bidder’s proposals, including compliance with the Bid Invitation Specification (BIS) and latest draft EPC Contract requirements, to help select a Preferred Bidder. This leads, after contract execution, to oversight of the EPC contractor’s nuclear and conventional engineering activities. Thereafter, the role requires preparation for a smooth transition from project delivery into operations, guaranteeing that all engineering documentation and systems are ready for operator training, licensing, and eventual plant operation under a Chief Nuclear Officer (CNO) structure.
   

  

 This position requires both deep technical expertise in nuclear engineering and strong leadership to enforce informed customer principles, vendor oversight, and regulatory compliance on behalf of the Owner.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Technical Evaluation of the EPC Bids and Negotiation of Contractual Obligations: Supporting the technical evaluation of the EPC bidder’s proposals from an engineering integrity and design delivery perspective, in accordance with the Bid Evaluation Manual (BEM), including compliance with the BIS and the draft Proposal Version EPC Contract requirements. Coordination of Technical Queries opposite bidders to obtain a clear understanding of their proposals where not adequately described in the initial bid documentation. Solicit sufficient information from the Preferred Bidder’s proposals and through negotiations to create a robust set of contractual obligations for the engineering and design throughout the full EPC lifecycle process. Establish a clear understanding with the Preferred Bidder of Owner expectations in performing the ‘Informed Customer’ role as part of EPC Contractor Audit and Surveillance.
* Engineering Deliverables Oversight: Lead the Owner’s verification and oversight of engineering deliverables produced by the EPC Contractor across the full project lifecycle – from Reference Plant definition, KSA adaptations, early licensing and Unit Design Basis, through detailed design maturity stages, as well as procurement, manufacturing, construction, commissioning, and final as\-built documentation. Start by preparing and implementing a technical oversight strategy; validate opposite EPC Preferred Bidder ensuring the EPC Contract includes all the necessary deliverables and associated obligations, listed, characterised and sequenced, and that the Owner creates an oversight organisation suitable to perform the role. Ensure that each deliverable, or a representative subset dependent on the oversight strategy, is reviewed for adequacy, accuracy, and compliance with contract requirements and nuclear industry standards, with the Owner’s team confirming the completeness and quality of the EPC Contractor’s work.
* Licensing Interface Management: Maintain a formal interface with the Owner’s Licensing function to guarantee that engineering outputs from the EPC Contractor support the nuclear licensing process opposite the national nuclear regulator. Coordinate engineering activities so that designs, analyses, and technical documents provide the necessary input for licensing submissions (e.g. Safety Analysis Reports, regulatory filings) enabling the licensing team to submit high quality, approved\-first\-time regulatory submissions. Ensure design changes or updates are strictly controlled and communicated to the licensing team, supporting a licensing strategy that remains up\-to\-date and compliant with national regulatory requirements.
* Owner’s Scope Definition \& Technical Interfaces: Define and manage all technical interfaces between the Owner’s scope of work and the LNPP EPC Contract. Establish clear battery limits and terminal points between the Owner’s scope (such as site infrastructure, off\-site utilities, and external systems) and the EPC Contractor’s plant scope. Oversee the integration of these interfaces into the overall plant design, verifying those external systems (power, water, communications, etc.) are properly incorporated in a timely manner to minimise the prospect of contractual disputes with the EPC Contractor. Manage technical interface control throughout the project, chairing interface meetings with the EPC and other stakeholders, and enforce strict change control processes to handle interface changes or scope adjustments over the project lifecycle.
* Operations Readiness \& Capability Development: Ensure that engineering deliverables are suitable for the eventual transition from a project delivery model under the Chief Technical Office (CTO) to an operations readiness model under the Chief Nuclear Officer (CNO). Work closely with the operations capability development team to confirm that design outputs (system descriptions, drawings, equipment specs, etc.) will support operator licensing and training, the development of operating procedures, maintenance schedules, and surveillance/testing programs. This includes verifying that maintainability and operability are built into the design, and that final documentation (as\-builts, manuals, etc.) will enable a smooth commissioning, fuel loading, and operational ramp\-up for the new nuclear facility.
* Regulatory Compliance \& Quality Assurance: Oversee engineering activities with a constant focus on nuclear safety and regulatory compliance. Ensure all engineering work adheres to national nuclear regulations and international standards (IAEA guidelines and WANO good practices), and that the design conforms to the licensing basis and safety analysis. Coordinate with nuclear safety, quality assurance, and independent review teams to conduct design reviews, audits, and technical assessments. Address any findings to maintain compliance and to uphold the highest standards of nuclear safety culture and quality throughout the project.
* Vendor Oversight \& Stakeholder Coordination: Act, in harmony with owner project and contract management teams, as the lead technical liaison between the Owner and the EPC contractor (and other vendors or consultants). Assist project and contract management in assessing EPC Contractor engineering earned value and technical milestone achievements, thus verifying the veracity of invoicing and payments. Implement an “informed customer” approach on behalf of the Owner – maintaining sufficient technical knowledge and oversight to intelligently monitor and challenge the contractor’s engineering decisions when necessary. Manage technical discussions and resolve issues with the EPC contractor’s engineering lead and team, ensuring that contractual technical requirements are met. Collaborate with external stakeholders such as grid operators, fuel suppliers, and regulatory bodies on technical matters, representing the Owner’s interests and ensuring all stakeholder requirements are integrated into the design and execution of the project. Coordinate with the owner procurement function to prepare and implement a make/buy strategy for appointing and integrating Owner’s Engineer resources.
* Team Leadership \& Reporting: Provide leadership and guidance to the Owner’s engineering team (including any discipline leads, specialists, and Owner’s Engineers on the project). Assign tasks, monitor progress, and mentor team members to build the organization’s capability as an intelligent owner. Prepare and deliver regular engineering status reports, technical presentations, and decision papers to senior management and governance committees. Ensure that any technical risks or issues are clearly communicated and that decision\-makers have the information needed to support project objectives while maintaining safety and compliance.


**Here’s What You’ll Need:**
 Minimum education qualification level:
   

  

* Educational Background: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Nuclear Engineering, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or a closely related field. An advanced degree (MSc or PhD) in a relevant discipline is highly preferred. Professional engineering licensure Educational Background: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Nuclear Engineering, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or a closely related field. An advanced degree (MSc or PhD) in a relevant discipline is highly preferred. Professional engineering licensure or chartered status, and additional training in nuclear safety or project management (e.g. PMP, APM, ISO21500:2021\), would be an advantage.
* Familiarity with some or all of the following would be advantageous:
* IAEA safety standards (SSR/SSG/GSR series);
* IAEA project management/engineering guidance (NG\-G/NG\- T/NP\-T series);
* International regulatory frameworks (e.g. NRC 10 CFR 52, NRC RG 1\.70, UK ONR GDA Guides);
* International utility/vendor standards (EUR, ASME, IEEE, ACI);
* Systems\-engineering frameworks/Configuration Management (INCOSE, ISO) and;
* Quality \& Oversight (IAEA GS\-G\-3\.1/3\.5, WANO PO\&Cs, IAEA TECDOC\-1740\).


 Minimum Experience \& Skills:
   

  

* Experience: Extensive experience (ideally 20\+ years direct experience) in nuclear power plant engineering and project delivery. This should include significant involvement in nuclear new\-build projects, ideally with at least one full project lifecycle (from design through commissioning) preferably on the owner’s side. Prior experience in an Owner’s Engineer or oversight role is essential, giving the candidate a deep understanding of how to set and implement the strategy to manage and review EPC Contractor deliverables and performance.
* Technical Expertise: In\-depth knowledge of nuclear power plant systems, engineering design processes, and project lifecycle stages. The candidate should be highly familiar with nuclear plant design documentation, systems engineering, and the integration of multi\-disciplinary engineering work (civil, process, mechanical, electrical, I\&C, nuclear safety analysis). Experience overseeing design maturity progress (e.g. system design bases from DB0 to DBn) and ensuring design coherence and safety case alignment through construction and commissioning is crucial.
* Industry Standards \& Compliance: Strong understanding of nuclear industry regulations and standards, both national and international. The candidate must have knowledge aligned with IAEA and WANO guidelines, demonstrating the ability to apply nuclear safety standards, quality assurance requirements, and best practices throughout the project. Familiarity with the concept of an “informed customer” or “intelligent customer” is required – the candidate should be able to maintain technical oversight of vendors in line with these principles, ensuring the Owner’s responsibilities under the regulatory framework are fully met.
* Leadership \& Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to lead and develop high\-performing engineering teams in a matrix, multicultural project environment. The candidate should possess excellent leadership and interpersonal skills to effectively coordinate between the Owner’s internal departments (licensing, safety, operations, etc.), the EPC contractor’s team, regulatory authorities, and other stakeholders. Experience in managing complex technical interfaces and facilitating problem\-solving among diverse teams is essential.
* Project Management \& Communication: Demonstrated project and contract management skills, including developing contract specifications, planning and prioritizing engineering work scopes, managing technical risks, and supporting project controls with engineering inputs (schedule, deliverables tracking, etc.). The candidate should be adept at technical communication – capable of producing clear technical reports, review comments, and presentations for senior management and regulatory audiences. Excellent communication skills in English are required, with the ability to articulate complex engineering issues to non\-specialist stakeholders and to represent the project in meetings with high\-level officials and international experts.


**Our Culture:**
 Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion, and diversity. We put people at the heart of our business, and we genuinely believe that we all succeed by supporting one another through our culture of caring. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees.
   

  

 We aim to embed inclusion and diversity in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we are more connected, and if we are diverse, we’re more creative. We accept people for who they are, regardless of age, disability, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, mental health, race, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. This is reflected in our wide range of Global Employee Networks centred on inclusion and diversity.
   

  

 We partner with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain diverse talent. For greater online accessibility, please visit Promoting Equality \& Diversity in Jobs and Career \- VERCIDA to view and access our roles. As a Disability Confident employer, we will interview all disabled applicants who meet the minimum criteria for a vacancy. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role.
   

  

 Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. We welcome feedback on our recruitment process and if you need more from us before deciding to join us then contact Andrew Duckworth.

data.title: Senior Manager - Delivery - Commercial Office (CO105)
data.raw_snippet: The Senior Manager \- Delivery in the Commercial Office at Qiddiya Investment Company is responsible for overseeing and driving the successful delivery of commercial projects and initiatives. This role involves managing project schedules, budgets, risks, and stakeholder communications to ensure comm
data.jd_text:
The Senior Manager \- Delivery in the Commercial Office at Qiddiya Investment Company is responsible for overseeing and driving the successful delivery of commercial projects and initiatives. This role involves managing project schedules, budgets, risks, and stakeholder communications to ensure commercial objectives are met efficiently and effectively. The Senior Manager will lead cross\-functional teams, coordinate with internal and external partners, and implement best practices in project and commercial management to align with Qiddiya's strategic goals.
   

  

 Key responsibilities include developing and monitoring delivery plans, managing contract negotiations, mitigating risks, and ensuring compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements. The role demands proactive problem\-solving, leadership, and excellent communication skills to foster collaboration and drive project success.
   

  

 Roles and Responsibilities
   

  

* Lead the planning, execution, and delivery of commercial projects to meet scope, schedule, quality, and budget targets
* Develop and maintain comprehensive project delivery plans, ensuring alignment with Qiddiya's strategic objectives and commercial policies
* Manage contract administration, procurement processes, and supplier relationships to optimize commercial outcomes
* Identify, assess and mitigate risks and issues related to project delivery while maintaining compliance with legal and regulatory standards
* Collaborate effectively with cross\-functional teams including finance, legal, procurement, and operations to ensure integrated project delivery
* Provide leadership and mentorship to project team members, fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and continuous improvement
* Prepare and present reports and updates to senior management and stakeholders regarding project status, challenges, and risks
* Drive the adoption of project management best practices, tools, and methodologies within the Commercial Office
* Ensure stakeholder engagement and manage communications to maintain alignment and support throughout project phases


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Project Management, Finance, or a related field
* 10\+ years of progressive experience in project delivery and commercial management within large organizations, preferably in entertainment, infrastructure, or investment sectors
* Strong knowledge of contract management, procurement processes, and commercial negotiation skills
* Proven track record in successfully managing complex projects with multiple stakeholders and tight deadlines
* Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to lead cross\-functional teams
* Proficiency with project management tools such as Microsoft Project, Primavera, or similar software
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities, capable of making sound decisions under pressure
* Experience working within or alongside commercial offices, with a good understanding of financial and legal considerations in project delivery
* Project Management Professional (PMP) or equivalent certification is desirable
* Fluency in English is required; additional language skills are a plus


**Benefits**
 Offering a comprehensive compensation and benefits package.

data.title: Infrastructure Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**About Sifi**
 SiFi is a corporate expense management platform designed to empower finance and accounting teams with seamless control over corporate spending. Our platform allows companies to issue cards with specific spending restrictions, ensuring that funds are used efficiently and only for appr
data.jd_text:
**About Sifi**
 SiFi is a corporate expense management platform designed to empower finance and accounting teams with seamless control over corporate spending. Our platform allows companies to issue cards with specific spending restrictions, ensuring that funds are used efficiently and only for approved expenses.
   

  

**Position Overview**
 The Infrastructure Manager is a senior role that combines strong people and operational leadership with deep, hands\-on technical expertise in infrastructure and reliability engineering. This role reports directly to the Director of Technology and is responsible for leading the Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) team, driving infrastructure strategy, and maintaining full technical authority over SiFi’s systems.
   

  

 Unlike a purely administrative management role, the Infrastructure Manager is expected to possess and actively apply deep technical knowledge — participating in architecture decisions, contributing during critical incidents, and evaluating infrastructure solutions at an engineering level. This role ensures high system availability, robust security controls, full compliance with SAMA regulations, and cost\-optimized infrastructure operations.
   

  

**Requirements**
**Team Leadership \& Culture**
* Lead, mentor, and develop the SRE and infrastructure team; set clear career paths, goals, and performance expectations.
* Foster a culture of ownership, continuous improvement, and technical excellence aligned with SiFi’s values: Customer Centricity, Product\-First Mindset, and Data\-Driven Excellence.
* Partner with Product and Engineering teams to ensure infrastructure supports secure, reliable product delivery.


**Infrastructure Architecture \& Engineering**
* Own and actively contribute to infrastructure architecture decisions, including reviewing and designing cloud\-native and hybrid solutions to support scalability, security, and performance.
* Lead the hands\-on design, implementation, and maintenance of secure, scalable, and resilient cloud and hybrid infrastructures, including deployment pipelines and system hardening.
* Perform regular architecture and system health assessments to proactively identify risks, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities; translate findings into actionable engineering work.
* Act as a technical authority in infrastructure and DevOps, providing guidance across teams and ensuring alignment with SiFi’s long\-term technology strategy.


**Reliability \& Incident Management**
* Define and enforce SLAs, SLOs, and error budgets; ensure system performance meets or exceeds targets through proactive monitoring, alerting, and automation.
* Provide hands\-on support during critical incidents, including deep technical troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and system recovery — acting as the most senior technical responder when needed.
* Lead production readiness reviews, post\-incident reviews, and disaster recovery drills; maintain and validate business continuity frameworks.
* Track and meet clear reliability KPIs, including system uptime targets (e.g., \>99\.95%).


**Security \& Access Control**
* Develop and enforce robust access control processes; ensure system access is authorized, reviewed regularly, and provisioned or de\-provisioned in a timely manner.
* Collaborate with the Cybersecurity Department to align infrastructure security with internal policies and regulatory requirements.


**Regulatory Compliance**
* Ensure infrastructure operations comply with SAMA regulatory controls; serve as the primary infrastructure contact for regulators, ensuring timely reporting and transparent cooperation.
* Coordinate with Compliance, Risk, and Cybersecurity teams to close regulatory observations and audit findings promptly.
* Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date compliance documentation and evidence for audits and inspections.


**Cost \& Vendor Management**
* Own and optimize the budget for infrastructure, systems, and software licenses; identify cost\-saving opportunities through vendor management and efficient resource utilization.
* Manage relationships with cloud providers, infrastructure vendors, and license suppliers; ensure SLA adherence and value delivery.


**Reporting \& Communication**
* Report to the Director of Technology and relevant stakeholders on system availability, incidents, compliance posture, and infrastructure health.
* Communicate proactively with internal functions about infrastructure initiatives, risks, and issue resolution.


**Benefits**
**Experience**
* 8\+ years of experience in infrastructure engineering, SRE, or cloud operations, with at least 3–4 years in a management or senior technical leadership role overseeing infrastructure or SRE teams.
* Experience with payment systems or financial services infrastructure is preferred.


**Technical Skills**
* Deep technical knowledge of Linux/Unix systems, networking, and infrastructure\-as\-code tools (e.g., Terraform, Ansible) — with the ability to contribute at an engineering level when required.
* Hands\-on experience with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is required; familiarity with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) is a strong advantage.
* Proven expertise in designing, operating, and scaling secure, high\-availability cloud\-native and hybrid environments, with a strong focus on OCI services including Compute, Networking, Object Storage, IAM, and Security Zones.
* Strong working knowledge of containerization and Kubernetes (including OCI Container Engine for Kubernetes — OKE); able to make informed architecture decisions and guide the team on container orchestration, deployment strategies, and cluster management.
* Solid understanding of DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines — including pipeline design, automation, and integration with infrastructure provisioning — at an architecture and governance level.
* Hands\-on experience with observability and monitoring stacks; able to architect and enforce a comprehensive monitoring strategy covering infrastructure metrics, log management, alerting, and distributed tracing (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, ELK/OpenSearch, or equivalent).
* Strong knowledge of SRE principles, observability, ITSM practices, and cost management for infrastructure and licenses.
* Familiarity with Microsoft infrastructure and services, including Windows Server, Active Directory, and SQL Server — as part of the environment includes Windows\-based systems and Microsoft data platforms.
* Hands\-on experience implementing and managing system access controls aligned with security frameworks.


**Leadership \& Compliance**
* Demonstrated ability to manage, mentor, and grow a high\-performing technical team while maintaining the technical credibility to guide engineering decisions and drive quality.
* Proven track record mentoring engineering teams on reliability and infrastructure best practices.
* Deep familiarity with regulatory frameworks, including SAMA, and experience cooperating with regulators and internal compliance functions.

data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
 WSP in the Middle East is currently seeking an accomplished and experienced professional to join our team as an
 **Associate Director – Building Services** 
 at our distinguished engineering consultancy, based in Riyadh, KSA.
   

  

 The successful candidate will assume a pivotal role in providing leadership to the MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing) design team, contributing to business development activities, and ensuring the successful delivery of projects. We are specifically seeking an individual with a robust background in engineering consultancy, demonstrating substantial experience in MEP design. A minimum of 18 years of relevant experience is required, with a strong preference for candidates who have worked in the GCC region and successfully delivered projects in KSA.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
 As an Associate Director in WSP, you are expected to be experienced and involved in the following:
   

  

* Nurture client relationship and engage in work winning. Create strong connections with existing clients and acquire new connections with impactful clients in KSA.
* Act as a WSP ambassador, representing WSP to clients and their representatives.
* Hands\-on experience in the operational delivery of small to medium size projects as an MEP leader or multidisciplinary leader for all engineering and architectural services including management of subconsultants.
* Lead and successfully deliver small to medium size project proposals, MEP or multidisciplinary. Closely communicate with clients to secure projects.
* Have excellent communication skills. Have strong presentation skills and the ability to create meaningful connections with clients, stakeholders and peers at WSP.
* Building Services technical leader that is well rounded in all MEP services and understand each discipline's main constraints, risks and opportunities
* Have excellent technical knowledge in mechanical or electrical building services and delivered several projects successfully to the client’s satisfaction. Have a deep understanding of standards, regulations and authority requirements and can provide tailored advice to our clients.
* Skilled in delivering different project market sectors (Commercial, Retail, Hospitality, etc.) and have a strong understanding of associated standard, authority requirements and sustainability requirements
* Develop the technical capability of the team. Identify weaknesses and support them to improve their performance and outcome proactively and constructively
* People leader, able to lead, inspire, engage, and develop a team.
* High financial acumen with experience in budget management, change management and accountability for project financial performance, having a track record of delivering project within agreed design fee.
* Deliver high integrity documentations with focus on quality and meeting client intent.
* Supporting Team Leaders / Head of Building Services on project reviews including Technical and Commercial activities.
* Responsible for project technical control and ensure that all designs are technically sound and meet the client brief.
* Check project team's work thoroughly and ensure it complies to standards, authority requirements and client intent. Work closely with team to ensure the work is coordinated and correct
* Experienced in resource management and leading a large project team. Able to assess team capability, distribute tasks and control the flow of work.
* Excellent risk management skills, able to raise, control and mitigate risk effectively and in a timely manner.
* Review project commercials along with the stage deliverables on monthly basis and report to the project director and Team Leaders.
* Project financial control, end of month reporting on project financial status, possible VOs, invoicing status, WIP and debt.
* Ensure project management procedure is followed as per company practice including QA.
* Reporting to the direct report on project technical and commercial performance. Keep the project team informed of key deliverables, deadlines, program, progress, issues, and changes.
* Review and endorse MEP design documents to ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations.
* Establishing and monitoring project key performance indicators (KPIs) for the project to comply with all company policies, procedures and guidelines.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in building services with a mechanical or electrical bias; master’s degree is a plus.
* Preferably Chartered with a reputable professional body (CIBSE, IET, etc.)
* Minimum of 18 years of experience in MEP design, with a multi\-disciplinary consulting engineering background.
* A high sense of ownership and accountability
* Proven leadership experience with the ability to inspire, develop and motivate a large team.
* Out of box thinking and problem solving.
* In\-depth knowledge of MEP codes, standards, and best practices.
* Experience working in the GCC region is highly desirable.


 Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.
   

  

 Join our close\-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world.
   

  

 With us, you can. Apply today.

data.jd_text:
Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey.
   

  

**The Project**
 This is an opportunity to work on one of the largest programmes in Saudi Arabia. This unique project consists of major mixed\-use development including sports facilities, entertainment, and arts along with all associated infrastructure.
   

  

 Experienced Health and Safety Director (Associate Director grade) to support the delivery of a major giga programme, working directly with the client.
   

  

 The role will be embedded within a defined sub district environment and will provide strategic and operational HSE leadership across a multi project portfolio, leading a team of HSE professionals and influencing a wide range of stakeholders.
   

  

 This is a senior leadership position requiring a confident, credible individual who is equally effective on construction sites and in senior leadership forums, a true “boots and suits” role.
   

  

**You’ll Be Responsible For**
* Act as the senior HSE lead for the assigned sub district within the programme.
* Represent Mace in engagement with the client, providing trusted, authoritative HSE advice.
* Drive a strong, positive health, safety and wellbeing culture aligned to client expectations and Mace standards.
* Influence senior stakeholders and delivery teams to achieve consistently high HSE performance.
* Provide leadership and oversight across multiple concurrent projects, supported by a team of HSE Managers.
* Maintain a strong visible leadership presence on site, confidently engaging with contractors and project teams.
* Challenge unsafe behaviours, intervene where necessary, and ensure corrective actions are embedded.
* Ensure compliance with statutory requirements, client policies, and Mace governance.
* Review, analyse, and prepare HSE data, dashboards, reports, and trend analysis for internal and client use.
* Lead audits, inspections, and assurance activities across the portfolio.
* Lead and oversee incident investigations, ensuring robust root cause analysis and lessons learned.
* Prepare and deliver clear, professional presentations to senior leadership, translating operational performance into strategic insight.
* Line manage and mentor a team of HSE Managers, setting direction, priorities, and performance expectations.
* Build a high performing, collaborative HSE team capable of supporting a complex delivery environment.


**You’ll Need To Have**
* Proven experience at Senior Manager / Director / Associate Director level within major construction or development programmes.
* Strong track record delivering HSE leadership across large, multi project environments.
* Comfortable operating at both site level and executive level, with the credibility to influence at all tiers.
* Demonstrable experience leading investigations, producing senior level reports, and managing performance data.
* Experience managing and developing HSE teams.
* NEBOSH Diploma (or equivalent) – essential.
* Chartered status (IOSH or equivalent) – preferred.


**Our values**
**Safety first \- Going home safe and well:** 
 We champion a safe, diverse, and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of well\-being in every team.
   

  

**Client focus \- Deliver on our promise:** 
 We own the quality of deliverables and strategic outcomes and build long\-term relationships with our clients.
   

  

**Integrity \- Always do the right thing:** 
 We influence positive outcomes within our industry while always aligning with our compliance obligations.
   

  

**Create opportunity \- For our people to excel:** 
 We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.
   

  

 Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfill all the criteria, please apply, as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.
   

  

 We are also open to discussing part\-time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role.

data.jd_text:
**Company Description**
 Saatchi \& Saatchi is a leading global communications and advertising network known for its impactful creative ideas that generate powerful emotional connections between consumers and brands. Present in over 67 countries, Saatchi \& Saatchi employs 6,500 professionals over 114 offices. Global clients include HSBC, P\&G, Toyota, Visa and GSK. Saatchi \& Saatchi is part of Publicis Communications a key division of Publicis Groupe, the world’s third largest communications group.
   

  

**Overview**
 Work with wider team and client to handle day\-to\-day operations of client’s social activity.
   

  

 Key responsibilities include gathering insights to supercharge social ideas, recommending and managing social activity (and calendar) and delivering best\-in\-class social content
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Technical \& Product**
* Write, edit, and proofread content (captions) in both Arabic and English
* Vary copywriting voice, style, and other characteristics demanded by industry, target audience, social platform, and objective
* Publish content across different social media channels
* Set up social listening monitors \& conduct regular social listening exercises
* Perform quality assurance of community management and provide recommendations to client to improve community management response
* Create monthly competitive reports by monitoring social media activity of competition
* Track social content performance on the different channels and formulate monthly reports
* Assemble creative briefs for creative teams, within social media specifications


**Business Acumen \& Operational Efficiency**
* Engage with client stakeholders on daily basis
* Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of content and technology trends
* Execute projects with excellence
* Maintain positive relationships with all partners and teammates


 Innovative \& Analytical Mindset
   

  

* Report on and analyse competitor and brand activity
* Synthesise social sentiment for takeaways on brand perception
* Suggest ways to leverage latest content and technology trends to clients’ benefit
* Propose fresh ideas for social content


**Qualifications**
* 2\-4 years professional experience in similar role
* Bachelor’s degree in communication, journalism, business, or related field
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Experience with social listening tools
* Bi\-lingual \- Arabic and English fluency is a must
* Highly computer literate
* Knowledge of the different social media platforms and post formats


**Additional Information**
 R\-3597L\-1137

* Manage PMO and Project Controls performance on assigned commissions under senior guidance.

* Build trusted client and stakeholder relationships, fostering collaboration and confidence.
* Contribute to monthly reporting and performance reviews, ensuring clarity and accuracy.
* Recommend and develop methodologies, including Earned Value Management, to enhance outcomes.
* Integrate risk management and planning disciplines across PMO activities.
* Align all PMO and Project Controls activity with Mace Control Centre standards, client needs, and legislation.
* Provide technical expertise while supporting Mace’s net zero carbon transition.


**You’ll Need To Have**
* 7–15 years’ PMO or programme controls experience, delivering discipline excellence.
* Proven leadership of teams, with strong analytical and problem‑solving capability.
* Technical expertise in PMO/controls disciplines, supported by professional qualifications.
* Effective communicator, detail‑focused, adaptable, and proficient in Microsoft Office.


**Our values**
**Safety first \- Going home safe and well:** 
 We champion a safe, diverse and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of wellbeing in every team.
   

  

**Client focus \- Deliver on our promise:** 
 We own the quality of deliverables, strategic outcomes and build long term relationships with our clients.
   

  

**Integrity \- Always do the right thing:** 
 We influence positive outcomes within our industry, while always aligning with our compliance obligations.
   

  

**Create opportunity \- For our people to excel:** 
 We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.
   

  

 Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfil all the criteria, please apply as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.
   

  

 We are also open to discussing part time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable with\-in the role.
