data.title: Senior Manager - Design (Architecture) Sport BU (SPA4-SPO-014)
data.raw_snippet: Qiddiya Investment Company is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Senior Manager \- Design (Architecture) to join our Sports Business Unit. This pivotal role involves leading the architectural design efforts for world\-class sports facilities that align with Qiddiya's vision to create a premier e
data.jd_text:
Qiddiya Investment Company is seeking a highly skilled and motivated Senior Manager \- Design (Architecture) to join our Sports Business Unit. This pivotal role involves leading the architectural design efforts for world\-class sports facilities that align with Qiddiya's vision to create a premier entertainment and sports destination. You will oversee the architectural design process from concept to completion, ensuring innovative, functional, and sustainable design solutions that meet international standards.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* To provide architectural design management on the sports assets working closely with the Development Lead, Senior Director of Design, Project Design Director and the LDC's and their sub\-consultants.
* To ensure coordination of the design and engineering with all disciplines
* To closely work with the development team to align to budgets and project timescales
* To manage weekly design meetings
* To coordinate with the Master developer where required
* To ensure the assets meet all building codes including City Framework standards and other standards required by federations.
* To keep abreast of any changing codes and ensure they are adopted.
* To ensure the consultants meet their deliverables as per the contract
* To review all drawings / deliverables and approve or comment as required using the CRS process


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Architecture or related field
* At least 7\-15 years of professional experience in architectural design, with significant experience in sports facility design
* Strong knowledge of sports architecture requirements, including stadiums, arenas, and related infrastructure
* Proficiency in design software such as AutoCAD, Revit, Rhino, and other relevant tools
* Demonstrated ability to lead multidisciplinary design teams and manage complex projects
* Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills
* Experience working within the Middle East region is preferred
* Deep understanding of sustainable design principles and building codes

data.raw_snippet:

 We are looking for a Project Controls Manager on a large project in Riyadh, KSA. The role will be overseeing the schedule development, procurement, planning, progress monitoring and reporting for this real estate development.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities:
   

  

* Ma
data.jd_text:
Position Overview:
   

  

 We are looking for a Project Controls Manager on a large project in Riyadh, KSA. The role will be overseeing the schedule development, procurement, planning, progress monitoring and reporting for this real estate development.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities:
   

  

* Maintaining the schedule baselines and ensure project control mechanisms are in place to monitor performance.
* Set up, develop and control all platforms for project tracking and reporting.
* Manage schedule of a large project continually assessing complexity and risk of the project.
* Review and report on project metrics regularly and advise project leadership of issues.
* Investigate and analyse variances in data as needed.
* Coordinate with Contract Administrators in assessing extension of time claims, merits of claims, delay analysis,
* Prepare reports internally for the project leadership team and externally for the client and other stakeholders.


 Required Qualifications:
   

  

* Degree Certificate in engineering with minimum 15 years of experience in the field
* Min 5 years of experience in Gulf region
* P6 trained with minimum 5 years utilizing this software.
* Earned Value (EV) and Planned Value (PV) Reporting expert, able to cost load to a detailed WBS to enable financial reporting against programme and coordination with contracted terms and conditions.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Professional Memberships are an advantage.
* Extensive experience in managing Project Controls on large, mixed use construction projects in the MENA region.
* High calibre team leadership skills, managing multiple nationalities on a major programme.
* Excellent written and verbal skills with a fluent proficiency in English.

data.title: Director Investment & Business Development - Saudi National
data.raw_snippet: Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development
data.jd_text:
Egis is an international player active in architecture, consulting, construction engineering and mobility services. We create and operate intelligent infrastructures and buildings that respond to the climate emergency and contribute to more balanced, sustainable and resilient territorial development. Operating in 100 countries, Egis puts the expertise of its 19,500 employees at the service of its clients and develops cutting\-edge innovations accessible to all projects. Through its wide range of activities, Egis is a key player in the collective organisation of society and the living environment of citizens all over the world.
   

  





 With 3,500 employees across 8 countries in the Middle East, Egis has delivered over 700 complex development projects, stimulating economic growth and enhancing quality of life. Ranked among the top ten firms in the Middle East by Engineering News Record (ENR), Egis is committed to sustainable development. The Group’s operations in the Middle East are built on strategic acquisitions and a deep understanding of local market conditions. Egis’ long history of providing comprehensive engineering, consulting, and project management services makes it a trusted partner for regional governments, investors, and developers.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

 As the Investment and Business Development Director, you will spearhead the company's efforts in identifying and capitalizing on investment opportunities for sustainable urban development projects. Drawing upon your extensive experience in investment management and your deep understanding of the engineering industry, you will lead initiatives to attract funding, secure partnerships, and drive the financial success of our projects.
   

  





 The Investment and Business Development Director will work closely with the Investment Planning Director and the team.
   

  

  

  




**Qualifications**
* Investment and Business Development Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive Business development strategy aligned with projects objectives and market dynamics.
* Opportunity Identification: Conduct in\-depth market research and feasibility studies to identify potential investment opportunities in sustainable urban development projects.
* Investor Relations: Cultivate and maintain relationships with investors, financial institutions, and other stakeholders to secure funding and support for project development.
* Due Diligence: Conduct thorough due diligence on potential investment opportunities, including financial, legal, and technical assessments, to mitigate risks and ensure investment viability.
* Deal Structuring: Lead negotiations and structuring of investment deals, including equity investments, debt financing, joint ventures, and strategic partnerships.
* Compliance and Reporting: Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and reporting standards, and provide regular updates to stakeholders on investment performance and portfolio management.
* Team Leadership: Provide leadership and guidance to the investment development team, fostering a culture of excellence, collaboration, and innovation.


**Additional Information**
* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business Administration, or a related field; MBA or CFA designation preferred.
* Minimum of 15 years of experience in investment management, financial analysis, or corporate finance, with a focus on infrastructure or real estate investments.
* Strong understanding of investment principles, financial markets, and project finance structures.
* Proven track record of successfully sourcing, structuring, and closing investment deals, preferably in the infrastructure or real estate sector.
* Excellent analytical, quantitative, and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to assess complex investment opportunities and risks.
* Effective communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders.
* Strategic thinker with a results\-oriented mindset, capable of identifying and pursuing profitable investment opportunities.
* Proficiency in financial modeling and valuation techniques; familiarity with investment analysis software is a plus.
* Fluency in English and proficiency in Arabic \- reading and writing.

data.raw_snippet:

data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
   

  

 At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
   

  

**Job Description**
 We are seeking a proactive and results\-driven Saudi National
 **Senior Project Manager – Delivery** 
 to lead the safe, timely, and efficient delivery of a major infrastructure project. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing all aspects of the project lifecycle, including planning, execution, monitoring, and closeout, while ensuring alignment with project objectives, timelines, and budgets.
   

  

 Candidates must have extensive experience working in the concept, design and procurement phases of the project lifecycle along with previous experience of working in a Project Management or Engineering Consultancy.
   

  

 This role requires strong leadership, stakeholder engagement, and the ability to manage complex project scopes while maintaining compliance with health, safety, and environmental (HSE) standards, quality assurance, and performance objectives. The ideal candidate will have extensive experience in managing multidisciplinary teams, overseeing scope management, and driving project delivery in collaboration with contractors, consultants, and stakeholders.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
**Health, Safety, and Security:**
* Champion a culture of health, safety, wellbeing, and security across the project, ensuring compliance with organizational policies, client requirements, and local regulations.
* Conduct safety investigations, inspections, and audits as required to maintain a safe working environment.


**Project Planning And Execution**
* Develop and implement robust project delivery plans, covering all aspects of project preparation, execution, and closeout.
* Monitor and report on project progress, ensuring alignment with time, cost, HSE, quality assurance, and performance objectives.
* Identify and assess risks and opportunities, develop mitigation strategies, and manage them through regular reviews.


**Stakeholder Engagement**
* Act as the primary point of contact for internal and external stakeholders, fostering collaboration and resolving conflicts.
* Ensure a ‘Customer\-first’ approach is integrated into all aspects of project planning, decision\-making, and delivery.
* Collaborate with local authorities, contractors, and key third parties to ensure alignment with project objectives.


**Scope And Change Management**
* Oversee scope management, constructability reviews, and contract alignment to ensure project objectives are met.
* Manage changes through defined change management processes, ensuring early identification and communication of potential impacts.


**Design And Procurement Coordination**
* Collaborate with design and engineering teams to ensure the successful delivery of concept and detailed designs.
* Support procurement processes by developing detailed scopes of work, facilitating market engagement, and evaluating contractor and supplier proposals.


**Procurement And Commercial Support**
* Lead the procurement process by selecting appropriate contract models, developing scopes of work, facilitating market engagement, and conducting rigorous reviews to engage suitable contractors and consultants.
* Work effectively with the commercial team to ensure contractual compliance and cost control are maintained to the highest level.


**Team Leadership And Mentorship**
* Lead and mentor a multidisciplinary team, fostering a high\-performance culture through clear direction, continuous feedback, and recognition of exceptional performance.
* Provide coaching and professional development opportunities to support the growth of team members.


**Risk And Issue Management**
* Identify, assess, and mitigate project risks, developing strategies to address roadblocks and maintain project momentum.
* Conduct post\-project reviews to capture lessons learned and identify opportunities for improvement.


**Quality Assurance And Compliance**
* Ensure all project deliverables meet quality standards, technical specifications, and regulatory requirements.
* Implement and maintain project management processes, tools, and best practices to ensure transparency, standardization, and continuous improvement.


**Reporting And Communication**
* Prepare and deliver regular project updates, reports, and presentations to stakeholders and senior management.
* Maintain transparency and accuracy in project documentation and communication.


**Education**
**What Required Skills You'll Bring:**
* Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Project Management, Construction Management, or a related field.
* Higher National Diploma in a relevant field is also acceptable.


**Certifications**
* Project Management Professional (PMP), PRINCE2, AgilePM, or equivalent certifications are desirable but not mandatory.


**Experience**
* Minimum of 10\+ years of experience in project management, leading complex projects from initiation to completion within a contracting or construction environment.
* Proven ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, effectively controlling scope, schedule, budget, and resources.


**Technical Skills**
* Proficiency in enterprise\-level project management tools such as Microsoft Project, Primavera, or Aconex.
* Strong understanding of infrastructure delivery processes, including design, procurement, and construction.


**Soft Skills**
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
* Strong problem\-solving, decision\-making, and conflict resolution abilities.
* Ability to mentor and coach team members, fostering a collaborative and high\-performing environment.


**What Desired Skills You'll Bring**
* Must be fluent in English, both written and spoken, with excellent technical writing skills as well as oral and written communication skills.
* Proactive and delivery\-focused, with a strong ability to align stakeholders around a common vision.
* Flexible and adaptable to changing business needs, with the ability to prioritize workloads and meet tight deadlines.
* Skilled in managing teams and delivering results in a collaborative, cross\-departmental environment.
* Strong organizational and leadership skills, with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation.
* Experience working in Saudi Arabia and/or the Gulf region is highly preferred.


 Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
   

  

 We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
   

  

 Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent\-recruitment/.

data.raw_snippet: **Qiddiya is Saudi Arabia's future capital of entertainment, sports, and culture — a 360 km² mega\-project just outside Riyadh. From F1\-grade racetracks and theme parks to stadiums and performance venues, we're creating a destination like no other. This is more than a project, it's a national trans
data.jd_text:
**Qiddiya is Saudi Arabia's future capital of entertainment, sports, and culture — a 360 km² mega\-project just outside Riyadh. From F1\-grade racetracks and theme parks to stadiums and performance venues, we're creating a destination like no other. This is more than a project, it's a national transformation. As construction accelerates, we're hiring ambitious talent to help shape it. If you're driven by scale, impact, and the chance to build something iconic from the ground up, Qiddiya is calling for you.**
**General Summary:**
 The Senior Director \- Cost Reporting \& Controls provides Qiddiya\-wide leadership over cost reporting, governance, and internal controls. The role ensures accurate, timely, and decision\-ready cost and performance information for Executive Management and the Board, while strengthening cost discipline, transparency, and financial governance across all projects and business units.
   

  

 Applications closing 9th March 2026\.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
**Strategic Leadership in Cost Controls**
* Develop and implement a comprehensive cost reporting and control strategy for Qiddiya, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and governance frameworks
* Serve as the principal authority on cost transparency, performance monitoring, and financial accountability


**Standardization \& Reporting Frameworks**
* Create standardized methodologies for cost reporting, including templates, KPIs, and dashboards applicable to all projects and programs
* Guarantee the uniform application of reporting standards throughout Qiddiya


**Periodic \& Board\-Level Reporting**
* Deliver timely, precise, and insightful monthly, quarterly, and annual reports on cost and performance for Executive Leadership and Board Committees
* Provide comprehensive executive commentary, trend analysis, and forward\-looking insights


**Data Integrity, Systems \& Compliance**
* Implement strong validation and reconciliation processes across systems, including SAP, PRISM, Unifier, and BI tools
* Ensure adherence to audit requirements, internal policies, and governance standards


**Internal Controls, Audit \& Policy Oversight**
* Design, execute, and regularly evaluate a robust internal control framework for cost reporting processes
* Serve as the senior stakeholder for internal audits and oversee the remediation of identified control deficiencies


**Governance \& Performance Monitoring**
* Conduct monthly reviews of cost performance
* Ensure the timely escalation of variances, risks, and issues, alongside proactive recommendations to Committees


**Stakeholder Engagement \& Leadership Interface**
* Act as the primary point of contact with Executive Leadership and cross\-functional stakeholders
* Influence strategic decisions by leveraging insights from cost performance


**Talent Management \& Organizational Development**
* Lead workforce planning, recruitment, and development initiatives for cost reporting and control teams
* Foster strong analytical and governance capabilities within the organization


**Continuous Improvement \& Innovation**
* Advocate for automation, advanced analytics, and the integration of innovative technologies
* Collaborate with IT and Digital teams to establish a single source of truth and enhance cost governance maturity


**Reporting Line**
 Reports to: Head of CDU Finance and Commercial.
   

  

**Requirements**
* Minimum of 13 years of experiences
* Bachelor's degree
* Strong negotiation and contract management skills
* Excellent leadership and team management capabilities
* Effective communication and interpersonal skills

 At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Parsons is looking for a highly experienced
 **Senior Construction Director** 
 to join their team! The
 **Senior Construction Director** 
 will play a pivotal leadership role in the delivery of one of the world’s largest and most complex urban developments. Operating at true mega\-project scale, this role is responsible for overseeing the construction of major infrastructure networks, high\-quality public realm, and iconic Tier 1 architectural assets.
   

  

 The position requires strategic oversight of multiple PMCM consultants and contractors, ensuring alignment with the client’s vision, program objectives, and world\-class quality expectations. The role will act as a key interface between the client, delivery partners, and senior stakeholders, driving performance across technical, commercial, and operational functions to ensure successful project execution.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing;**
* Provide executive\-level leadership for construction delivery across multiple zones/packages within the program.
* Oversee the performance of PMCM consultants and contractors, ensuring adherence to schedule, budget, quality, and safety objectives.
* Drive integrated delivery across infrastructure, public realm, and complex building assets.
* Ensure alignment of construction activities with the approved masterplan, phasing strategy, and operational requirements.
* Act as a primary point of contact for the client on all construction\-related matters.
* Lead high\-level coordination with client representatives, government authorities, and key stakeholders.
* Provide clear, accurate, and timely reporting on project status, risks, and mitigation strategies.
* Support executive decision\-making through strategic advice and data\-driven insights.
* Manage and direct PMCM consultants, ensuring effective delivery of construction management services.
* Challenge and guide PMCMs on technical, sequencing, constructability, and risk\-related matters.
* Ensure consistency in standards, processes, and reporting across all delivery partners.
* Lead resolution of complex technical and coordination issues across multiple disciplines.
* Monitor and drive progress against program milestones, proactively addressing delays and constraints.
* Identify and manage key project risks, implementing mitigation and recovery strategies where required.
* Oversee construction sequencing and logistics planning at a program\-wide level.
* Support commercial teams in managing contractor performance, variations, and claims.
* Ensure construction delivery aligns with contractual obligations and procurement strategies.
* Contribute to dispute avoidance and resolution strategies.
* Lead, mentor, and develop a high\-performing, multidisciplinary construction team.
* Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and excellence.
* Ensure appropriate resource planning and capability within the team.
* Champion best\-in\-class HSE standards and ensure compliance across all works.
* Drive quality assurance and control processes to deliver world\-class assets.
* Support sustainability objectives in line with project and national goals.


**What Required Skills You'll Bring;**
* Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).
* Minimum of 20\+ years of experience in construction delivery, with at least 10 years in senior leadership roles on large\-scale or mega projects.
* Proven track record in delivering complex infrastructure, public realm, and high\-profile architectural developments (Tier 1 or landmark assets).
* Extensive experience managing PMCM consultants and Tier 1 contractors in a client\-facing role.
* Strong knowledge of international construction standards, contract forms (e.g., FIDIC), and delivery models.
* Demonstrated ability to manage large, multidisciplinary teams and multiple stakeholders.
* Experience in the Middle East, particularly in KSA giga\-projects, is highly desirable.
* Professional certifications (e.g., PMP, MRICS, PE) are advantageous.


 Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.

data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
**What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?**
 At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates ne
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?**
 At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.
   

  

 WSP is seeking an experienced Project Manager to play a pivotal role in driving both work winning and operational delivery across the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. This strategic role brings together business development and project execution, contributing directly to the organisation’s growth and long term success in the region.
   

  

 The successful candidate will be responsible for identifying and securing new opportunities, building and maintaining strong client relationships, and ensuring the effective mobilisation and delivery of projects from award through to completion.
   

  

 As a key driver of growth and delivery performance, the Project Manager will lead capture strategies and tender submissions for major public and private sector clients, while maintaining oversight of project execution, commercial performance, and delivery outcomes post award.
   

  

**Key requirements include:**
* Strong commercial acumen and contractual awareness.
* In\-depth knowledge of the Saudi construction market.
* Proven experience in winning and delivering major projects.
* Ability to operate strategically while retaining hands on delivery oversight.


 This role offers the opportunity to work on high profile projects while playing a central role in shaping the organisation’s continued growth across Saudi Arabia.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
**Work Winning / Business Development (Pre\-Contract)**
* Lead the full bid lifecycle: EOI, prequalification, tender review, proposal strategy, preparation, and submission.
* Develop and execute capture strategies for key clients including ministries, PIF entities, and major private developers.
* Review tender documents in detail to identify scope gaps, commercial risks, pricing exposure, and contractual obligations.
* Lead the development of pricing strategies aligned with market conditions, competitive positioning, and company margin targets.
* Perform cost benchmarking against historical data and current market rates.
* Lead internal pricing reviews and sign\-off meetings with senior management.
* Participate in commercial negotiations, clarifications, and value engineering discussions with clients.
* Maintain a bid cost database and lessons\-learned register to improve future pricing accuracy and win rates.
* Support contract finalization to ensure alignment between tender pricing assumptions and awarded scope


**Project Operations (Post\-Contract)**
* Lead project mobilization in accordance with contractual scope, deliverables, and agreed commercial terms.
* Ensure thorough contract review upon award to validate scope boundaries, pricing assumptions, exclusions, and risk allocation.
* Administer contracts in line with contract conditions, ensuring compliance with all contractual obligations.
* Establish project execution plans, cost baselines and resource deployment forecasts.
* Oversee project P\&L performance, including revenue recognition, gross margin protection, and cost control.
* Update cost\-to\-complete each month for assigned projects.
* Manage invoicing processes, ensuring timely submission, certification, and collection in coordination with finance.
* Lead commercial management of variations, scope changes, and additional services.
* Identify and proactively manage commercial risks, claims exposure, and dispute matters.
* Ensure proper documentation of correspondence, instructions, approvals, and change records to protect contractual position.
* Manage subconsultant agreements including scope definition, performance monitoring, certification of payments, and back\-to\-back commercial alignment.
* Conduct regular project performance reviews with senior management.
* Close out projects commercially and contractually, including final account settlement and lessons\-learned documentation.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Civil, Structural, Mechanical, Electrical), Construction Management, Quantity Surveying, or related discipline.
* PMP certification or equivalent preferred.
* Minimum 8 years of experience within engineering consultancy or construction management.
* Proven experience in both bid management and project delivery in Saudi Arabia.
* Good knowledge of Saudi construction industry, clients and procurement processes.
* Experience working on major infrastructure, mixed\-use, or giga\-projects.
* Excellent communications and negotiation skills.
* Fluency in English required; Arabic strongly preferred.


**Competencies**
* Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen.
* Leadership capabilities.
* Strong analytical and risk management skills.
* High level of professionalism and client\-facing confidence.
* Ability to work in a fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environment.


**Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.**
 Join our close\-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world.
   

  

**With us, you can. Apply today.**

 The HR team at A\&O Shearman provides a first class service to the business, offering strategic HR business advice at the business partner level and quality support services in the full range of HR functions through specialist teams such as, learning \& development, resourcing, reward, employee relations advice, payroll, systems and HR administration.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 Support the Head of HR in the provision of strategic and operational advice and support to relevant client groups and to assist in the delivery of the strategic HR plan for the Middle East region.
   

  

 Develop and manage effective working relationships with key stakeholders. Work as a fully integrated and trusted advisor to the partners and support heads.
   

  

 Resourcing;
   

  

* Management of all staff movements (leavers, joiners, secondments etc)
* Work closely with partners to understand recruitment requirements for the Riyadh office
* Liaise with external recruitment market as required
* Owning the full cycle of the recruitment process for fee earners and business teams, including lateral recruitment and talent attraction
* Work closely with the UAE graduate recruitment manager to develop and implement the KSA graduate recruitment strategy
* Input into the client and international secondment planning process including identification of suitable secondees


 Training and Development;
   

  

* Work with the UAE HR team to develop the training and development offering for the Riyadh office
* Support with the coordination of any training held locally
* Develop and manage regional training needs analysis
* Manage the induction process, including coordinating the relevant team members, for the Riyadh office


 Performance management
   

  

* Successfully embedding Compass into the practice groups and business teams, ensuring regular feedback and check\-ins take place and all parties are appropriately briefed and trained
* Supporting partners and managers to deliver effective staff management processes
* Supporting quarterly talent discussions and ensuring that development outcomes arising from talent discussions and check\-ins are actioned
* Analysis and action planning following engagement surveys
* Ensuring diversity and wellbeing initiatives are implemented across the practice groups \& support teams


 Payroll and reward;
   

  

* Work with wider HR team to ensure Global HR Systems are updated accordingly
* Manage the payroll process for the Riyadh office with the external payroll provider
* Supporting local management and the Head of HR to develop and implement reward policies for the Riyadh office
* Management, together with the UAE HR team, of the Riyadh office annual salary and bonus review process


 HR Administration;
   

  

* Be the custodian of HR systems, including PeopleSoft for the Riyadh office
* Employment contract management
* Drafting documents in English and Arabic as required
* Work closely with/ support the PRO team with all relevant documentation for local government / authority purposes
* Administration of employee requests including no objection letters, housing loans, references etc.
* Manage leaver administration; end of service benefits etc.


 Policy/ER;
   

  

* Be the ‘go to’ for local employment policy and legislative framework advice for the KSA office
* Manage the maternity leave process
* Conduct exit interviews, as appropriate
* Manage the administration of the annual health insurance renewal process and health insurance related queries throughout the year
* Update GOSI and related systems according to the requirements
* Manage the administration of periods of unpaid leave, flexible working arrangements etc.
* Monitor sickness absence, advising line managers and supervisors where sickness levels are of a concern


**What You Will Have**
* Must be resilient, pro\-active and have excellent interpersonal and communication skills
* Strong work ethic with the ability to cope well under pressure and display flexibility in approach
* Ability to adapt style as appropriate
* Excellent attention to detail, time management and organisational skills
* Strong client focus and able to build strong client relationships
* Technical competence in all aspects of KSA employment law, compliance practices, policies and GOSI
* Ability to work as part of a virtual team
* Must have relevant HR experience, preferably in a professional services environment
* Collaborative team player with the ability to work in a fast paced, intellectually rigorous environment
* A person of sound judgment, able to establish a high level of credibility in the organisation and act as a trusted advisor


**About A\&O Shearman**
 A\&O Shearman is a new global industry\-leading law firm, with nearly 50 offices in 29 countries worldwide. Our fluency in English law, US law, and the laws of the world’s most dynamic markets, enables us to provide unmatched insight and seamless delivery to clients. We work on challenging and important deals and disputes that have the potential to shape the future.
   

  

 We offer exceptional opportunities for our people; opportunities to work for the world’s leading businesses; to transform the status quo, and to deliver your best work, helping you and your career to thrive, while delivering unparalleled outcomes for our clients.
   

  

 Whether you're helping clients solve complex challenges, transforming the ways we manage our business, or ensuring the smooth\-running of our operations, this is an environment where you can belong and excel. We provide first\-rate training and development, we are committed to an inclusive environment, and we provide support and ways of working that help you optimise your wellbeing.
   

  

 What truly defines a career with us? We recruit the best and ask for the best of you. And together, we will redefine success.

data.title: Country Head – Saudi Arabia, Senior Vice President
data.raw_snippet:
**Country Head/CEO – Saudi Arabia, Senior Vice President**
**Purpose, Key Responsibilities, And Scope Of The Role**
* Drive and execute State Street’s Investment Services and Investment Management strategy in Saudi Arabia by establishing, maintaining and growing strong local relationships and partne
data.jd_text:
**Country Head/CEO – Saudi Arabia, Senior Vice President**
**Purpose, Key Responsibilities, And Scope Of The Role**
* Drive and execute State Street’s Investment Services and Investment Management strategy in Saudi Arabia by establishing, maintaining and growing strong local relationships and partnerships with clients and prospects, ensuring delivery of high quality service and maintenance and evolution of strong product solutions to clients as a foundation for future growth.
* Act as CEO of State Street’s (SSSAFS) CMA regulated legal entity which houses both the IS and IM business, ensuring compliance with regulatory and legal expectations as set out by law and regulatory guidance and dialogue with all relevant stakeholders in the Kingdom.
* Execute oversight of all State Street divisions in Saudi Arabia (including the CRD strategy/staff as part of the legal entity)), ensuring best practices are shared and observed, and that all entities meet legal entity and corporate governance standards
* Manage the State Street country relationship with and play an active and effective advocacy role with the CMA, Ministry of Investment, Ministry of the Economy and Planning as well as with relevant stakeholders at the Royal Court, public agencies and acting as the public face and voice of State Street in the market
* Oversee the execution of the 5 year Strategic Plan of One State Street which includes to triple revenues in the next 5 years (end 2028\) establish locally domiciled ETF’s, Saudi Sub\-Custody, Fund Administration and an optimized UCITS Fund environment/Infrastructure to compete over the mid/long term with established centres around the world.
* Leverage established Network of both Private and Public Sector Relationships for the benefit of Vision 2030 and State Street as a Firm in Saudi playing a role in the Kingdom’s overall development.


**Managerial Responsibilities**
* Execute a country\-level strategy for State Street’s IS \& IM businesses in Saudi and cultivate a “One State Street” culture across all groups
* Act as local owner of services delivered by State Street in the Kingdom, working with functional leadership and global client executives and relationship managers as appropriate to ensure that our clients receive and experience an excellent and market\-leading level of service, taking local ownership of significant client\-related projects as appropriate (e.g. remediation program, new business acquisition and onboarding)
* Develop and cultivate relationships with locally\-based clients, management companies, Government and Government related entities as well as private Sectors Executives.
* Working with Product, Sales and Relationship Management leadership, develop and actively manage a pipeline of new business prospects and cross\-sells of new and emerging products and solutions to existing clients
* Working with GA/CCO/Global Delivery leadership, ensure the Saudi team meet client service, operational risk management and local regulatory requirements and expectations, especially concerning outsourcing and any emerging issues signalled by the regulator
* Contribute to and meet/where possible exceed sales and expense management financial targets
* Drive employee engagement and related initiatives across all locations to attain corporate expectations and advocate for actions that enhance the employer value proposition including measures that enhance Inclusion, Diversity \& Equity, as well as narrowing of the gender pay gap.
* Act as CEO of State Street Saudi Arabia Financial Solutions, ensuring the entity is managed and governed in line with company law, Saudi regulatory standards (as applicable) and State Street’s legal entity governance standards
* Lead and chair as appropriate the Country Leadership team and relevant risk and legal entity committees
* Represent State Street Saudi on regional and global committees and working groups as required
* Oversee the corporate functions teams in Saudi – those supporting the underlying business contracted from Saudi Arabia and centres of excellence (e.g. cyber security center)
* Ensure all Saudi legal entities adopt corporate governance and organizational standards commensurate with their size, complexity and regulatory rating
* Drive and act as a role model for cultures and behaviours in Saudi Arabia consistent with enterprise standards
* Develop and enhance through relevant dialogue and industry forums State Street’s relationship with regulators and government/public policy makers and their agencies
* Represent State Street in the business community and in industry organizations, ensuring our profile and reputation are enhanced


**About State Street**
 Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.
   

  

 We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.
   

  

 As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.
   

  

 Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers
   

  

 Read our CEO Statement

data.title: Senior SME – Merchant Acquiring Transformation
data.raw_snippet:
**About Us**
 Specialized, disciplined, and driven by measurable growth across every engagement.
   

  

 Ektis, founded in 2025, is a financial services consulting firm built on decades of combined industry experience. Our diverse team of specialists brings deep sector expertise across the regiona
data.jd_text:
**About Us**
 Specialized, disciplined, and driven by measurable growth across every engagement.
   

  

 Ektis, founded in 2025, is a financial services consulting firm built on decades of combined industry experience. Our diverse team of specialists brings deep sector expertise across the regional financial ecosystem, enabling us to turn complex challenges into practical, high\-impact solutions.
   

  

 As an expert\-led, boutique firm, we operate as an extension of our clients \- partnering closely with financial institutions to translate ambition into measurable progress. We focus on building enduring internal capability and applying creative problem\-solving that delivers lasting impact.
   

  

 Our approach integrates real\-world insights with strategic thinking and disciplined execution. We define objectives with precision, map the pathways required, and deliver outcomes that work in practice \- not just in presentation decks. Every deliverable is designed to be actionable, professional, and reflective of the capability and results our clients aim to achieve.
   

  

**Our Vision**
 Redefining financial services consulting for a new era \- accelerating ideas, deepening execution, and driving outcomes that matter.
   

  

**Our Mission**
 We partner with financial institutions to turn ambition into measurable results. Guided by our entrepreneurial spirit, we integrate with client teams and combine strategic clarity, disciplined execution, and creative problem\-solving to deliver lasting impact and build capabilities that endure
   

  

**About The Opportunity**
 Our client is a leading player within the Saudi payments market. As part of this engagement, Ektis is seeking a Senior SME to support the next phase of the programme.
   

  

 This is a highly hands\-on role for an experienced payments professional with experience across merchant acquiring, payment gateways and fintech acquiring set\-up. The successful candidate will bring a blend of commercial, technical and regulatory expertise, with the ability to support the design, launch and scaling of an acquiring business within Saudi Arabia.
   

  

**Role Focus**
 To act as the lead SME across the merchant acquiring transformation programme, providing strategic guidance and hands\-on support across the commercial model, technology architecture and Saudi regulatory requirements.
   

  

 The role will work closely with senior client stakeholders and the Ektis project team to help shape and deliver a modern acquiring proposition, ensuring it is commercially viable, technically robust and fully compliant with local regulations.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Commercial \& Business**
* Define the commercial strategy and business model for the acquiring proposition.
* Shape the merchant services offering, including target customer segments, proposition, pricing and go\-to\-market approach.
* Develop the target operating model, including sales channels, merchant onboarding, partnerships and service structure.
* Build commercial forecasts, revenue models and business plans.
* Advise on acquiring economics, including interchange, scheme fees, merchant pricing and profitability.
* Identify partnership opportunities with banks, fintechs, payment gateways and payment service providers.
* Support the roadmap for launching and scaling the acquiring business.


**Technology \& Platform**
* Define the target technology architecture for the acquiring and payment gateway platform.
* Lead or advise on the technical set\-up, integration and implementation of acquiring systems.
* Support build versus buy decisions and the selection of technology vendors and partners.
* Design the end\-to\-end payments architecture, including integration with banks, card schemes, processors and merchant systems.
* Provide expertise across payment gateways, merchant acquiring platforms, POS and SoftPOS, settlement, reconciliation and fraud systems.
* Ensure the solution is scalable, secure and aligned to PCI DSS, cybersecurity and data protection requirements.


**Regulatory \& Compliance**
* Advise on the Saudi regulatory requirements for establishing and operating a merchant acquiring business.
* Support the preparation and submission of acquiring license applications where required.
* Ensure the acquiring model and operating framework are aligned with local legal and regulatory obligations.
* Provide guidance across SAMA requirements, KYC, AML, merchant onboarding, transaction monitoring and settlement processes.
* Identify regulatory risks and recommend appropriate mitigation actions.
* Support engagement with regulators and key stakeholders across the Saudi payments ecosystem.


**Candidate Profile**
* Minimum 7 years' experience within payments, merchant acquiring, payment gateways or fintech acquiring set\-up.
* Proven experience helping banks, fintechs or payment providers establish, launch or transform acquiring businesses.
* Strong understanding of both the commercial and technical components of acquiring and payments.
* Deep familiarity with the Saudi payments landscape and regulatory environment.
* Experience working with SAMA, acquiring license processes and broader Saudi payments regulation is highly desirable.
* Background from Visa, Mastercard, a leading fintech, merchant acquirer, payment gateway provider or major payments organisation is strongly preferred.
* Acquiring Technology or Commercial Lead
* Strong understanding of merchant acquiring economics, payment gateway infrastructure, scheme integration and acquiring technology architecture.
* Existing experience within Saudi Arabia or the GCC is strongly preferred.
* Previous experience in roles such as: Head of Acquiring, Head of Merchant Services, Payments Transformation Lead, Senior Payments Consultant.


**Key Skills**
* Ability to operate at both strategic and hands\-on delivery level.
* Strong stakeholder management skills with experience engaging senior leadership.
* Ability to work across commercial, technology and regulatory workstreams simultaneously.
* Excellent communication, presentation and influencing skills.
* Proven experience within fast\-paced, transformation\-led environments.
* Experience supporting greenfield or large\-scale payments transformation programmes.


**Engagement Details**
* Senior advisory / consulting engagement through Ektis
* Project\-based appointment
* Based in Saudi Arabia or the GCC, with travel as required
* Immediate or short\-notice availability preferred

 Job Summary

data.jd_text:
Sulzer is a leading engineering company with a proud heritage of innovation. Join our global team to grow your expertise and develop innovative solutions that enable a prosperous and more sustainable society.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Job Summary
   

  

 The Head of Procurement is responsible for ensuring efficient and reliable supply of materials to support optimal production processes. The role is accountable for on\-time material availability, fulfillment of quality requirements, delivery performance (OTD), and cost optimization. This position works closely with Regional Procurement, Category Managers, and the local management team to ensure alignment between procurement activities and business objectives.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Responsibility for the procurement activities of all direct materials and defined indirect materials in SSPC
* Management of the local team of operational and category buyers
* Alignment and challenging of commodity strategies with category managers and the Management Team
* Support the coordination of procurement optimization activities across EMEA
* Support purchasers in the optimization of procurement costs
* Procurement controlling for SSPC
* Support supplier management
* Proactively engage and support company initiatives designed to promote a culture of safe working, environmental protection and continual improvement.
* Remain accountable for the health, safety and welfare of their departmental workforce.
* Ensure compliance to systems and procedures developed for the health and safety of the workforce and environmental protection
* Ensure that Safe Systems of Work are developed and implemented for all activities, through risk assessment and operating procedures
* Visibly lead EH\&S improvement through observation, intervention and workplace tours.


**Qualifications / Requirements**
* Technical or commercial university degree
* Significant experience of working in an international matrix organization this could limit your choices too much
* Significant management experience in the procurement of an engineering company
* Proven skills in supply chain management, supplier development,
* Excellent leadership, management and technical skills
* Strong negotiation skills
* Analytical skills and structured work
* Excellent communication skills, motivation and a high level of personal responsibility
* Managing and motivating teams and internal and external stakeholders
* Being able to work within a matrix organization
* Drive for results
* Direct and indirect leadership skills
* Ensuring accountability and integrity
* High level of loyalty to the company
* Strategic agility and global mindset
* Decision making within a complex environment, taking care about local and EMEA targets
* Functional / Technical competency
* Interpersonal skills
* Good MS Office skills and knowledge of relevant IT solutions, in particular SAP (MM)


 What we offer you
   

  

* Excellent opportunities for development, along with a competitive salary and company bonus scheme.
* A great working environment.
* Work Location: Riyadh\- Industrial Area 2


 Sulzer is an equal opportunity employer. We believe in the strength of a diverse workforce and are committed to offering an inclusive work environment.
   

  

 We are proud to be recognized as a Top Employer 2026 in Brazil, Canada, China, Finland, Germany, Ireland, Mexico, Switzerland, South Africa, the UK and the USA.

data.jd_text:
**ob Title:** 
 Director of Marketing
   

  

**Location:** 
 Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
   

  

**Industry:** 
 Logistics \& Courier Services
   

  

**Employment Type:** 
 Full\-Time \| Permanent
   

  

**Experience:** 
 6\-10 Years
   

  

**Nationality:** 
 Saudi National Preferred
   

  

**THE OPPORTUNITY**
 UltimatePeaks is retained by one of Saudi Arabia's largest and most established logistics and courier groups — a market leader with a nationwide footprint spanning hundreds of service centers and operations extending across the GCC and beyond.
   

  

 The group is entering an accelerated growth phase and is seeking a Director of Marketing to lead its brand transformation and digital marketing agenda. This is a high\-impact role at the center of the company's ambition to evolve from a logistics operator into a recognizable, digitally\-native consumer brand — one that resonates with the next generation of Saudi customers.
   

  

**AT A GLANCE**
* Reports To: Head of Commercial
* Location: Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
* Industry: Logistics \& Courier Services
* Nationality: Saudi National Preferred
* Employment: Full\-Time \| Permanent
* Experience: 6\-10 Years


**WHY THIS ROLE STANDS OUT**
**Market leader.** 
 Join Saudi Arabia's most recognized name in courier and last\-mile delivery.
   

  

**Real transformation.** 
 This is not a maintenance role — you will build and own the brand identity for a company in active growth mode.
   

  

**Digital\-first mandate.** 
 The organization is fully committed to becoming a digital\-native brand. Snapchat, TikTok, Instagram — this is your canvas.
   

  

**Strategic access.** 
 Direct line to commercial leadership with full budget authority and agency management responsibility.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
**Brand Strategy \& Transformation**
* Define and execute a bold brand repositioning strategy that modernizes the company's identity across all touchpoints
* Develop a unified visual language applicable to digital, physical, fleet, and service center environments
* Own brand health metrics — awareness, sentiment, NPS — and drive continuous improvement


**Digital Marketing \& Social Media**
* Lead the social media strategy across Snapchat, Instagram, TikTok, X, and LinkedIn with Snapchat as the primary growth channel
* Build an always\-on Arabic\-first content engine that speaks authentically to Saudi consumers
* Drive influencer and creator partnerships, performance marketing, and app acquisition campaigns


**Commercial Marketing \& Campaigns**
* Design integrated campaigns aligned to revenue targets — B2C, B2B, and e\-commerce merchant segments
* Lead high\-impact seasonal activations: Ramadan, National Day, 11\.11, and more
* Own marketing attribution and KPI reporting with full commercial accountability


**Team \& Agency Leadership**
* Build and lead an internal marketing team spanning creative, digital, and content
* Manage agency relationships across creative, media, PR, and production
* Establish marketing operations discipline — budget, process, and reporting cadence


**Requirements**
**WHO YOU ARE**
 We are looking for a young, ambitious Saudi marketer — someone who does not just understand digital, but lives it. You grew up on Snapchat. You know what makes content travel in the Saudi feed. You have built brands, not just managed them.
   

  

* Experience: 6\-10 years in marketing with a strong digital and brand focus
* Digital Fluency: Native command of Snapchat, TikTok, Meta, and Google Ads ecosystems
* Brand Building: Proven track record leading brand transformation or major rebranding
* Market Knowledge: Deep understanding of Saudi consumer culture and social media behavior
* Languages: Arabic (native) and English (professional)
* Education: Bachelor's in Marketing, Communications, Business, or equivalent
* Highly Valued:
+ Strong familiarity with Saudi digital ecosystems and content dynamics
+ Experience operating in high\-growth, consumer\-facing environments
+ Ability to build culturally relevant campaigns that resonate locally


****Leadership Expectations****
* Operates with strong ownership and accountability
* Brings both commercial thinking and cultural intuition
* Able to move fast, challenge assumptions, and deliver measurable outcomes
* Builds brands that earn attention through relevance, not just spend


****Confidentiality****
 This search is conducted on a strictly confidential basis.
   

  

 Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
   

  

**Benefits**
* High\-impact role with direct visibility to senior leadership
* Opportunity to lead a large\-scale brand transformation in Saudi Arabia
* Shape a marketing function with real commercial responsibility

data.title: Account Director, Luxury Sales KSA
data.raw_snippet:
Hilton is one of the largest and fastest growing hospitality companies in the world, with more than 9,000 properties with more than 1,300,000 rooms in 120 countries and territories.
   

  

 In the 105\+ years since our founding, we have defined the hospitality industry and established a portfolio
data.jd_text:
Hilton is one of the largest and fastest growing hospitality companies in the world, with more than 9,000 properties with more than 1,300,000 rooms in 120 countries and territories.
   

  

 In the 105\+ years since our founding, we have defined the hospitality industry and established a portfolio of 25 world\-class brands, with the emergence of the Tempo brand and including our flagship Hilton Hotels \& Resorts brand, which is the most recognised hotel brand in the world. We have more than 200\+ million members in our award–winning customer loyalty programme, Hilton Honors
   

  

**Role Overview**
 The Account Director – Luxury is responsible for driving strategic sales growth across Saudi Arabia, with a strong focus on luxury travel partners, high\-value accounts, and government delegations. This role plays a critical part in strengthening Hilton Luxury Brands’ presence in the region by cultivating long\-term relationships, unlocking new business opportunities, and positioning our portfolio of luxury hotels globally.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Develop and manage a portfolio of luxury travel trade accounts across primarily the KSA and potentially middle east, with a focus on high\-potential and strategic partners
* Identify, onboard, and grow new luxury accounts in line with regional and global sales objectives
* Strengthen relationships with government entities, royal offices, and protocol departments to drive luxury delegation business internationally
* Represent Hilton Luxury Brands to key stakeholders, ensuring brand positioning aligns with the expectations of ultra\-high\-net\-worth and VIP clientele
* Work closely with global luxury hotels to align market needs, client preferences, and commercial opportunities
* Drive production, track performance, and deliver against agreed revenue targets
* Lead and support client engagement initiatives including FAM trips, luxury events, roadshows, and international trade shows
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with internal teams to ensure seamless client servicing and strong post\-sale execution
* Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and emerging opportunities within the luxury travel segment


**What are we looking for?**
**Key Skills \& Experience**
* Proven experience in luxury sales, account management, or luxury travel trade (hospitality experience preferred)
* Strong network within the Saudi luxury travel ecosystem
* Experience working with government, protocol, or high\-level delegation business is a strong advantage
* Excellent relationship\-building and stakeholder management skills
* Commercially driven with strong negotiation and presentation abilities
* Culturally aware, detail\-oriented, and comfortable engaging at senior and VIP levels
* Ability to work independently while contributing effectively within a regional and global team
* Ability and flexibility to travel.


**What Success Looks Like**
* Strong growth in luxury account production across Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
* Increased number of active, high\-quality luxury travel partners
* Expanded government and delegation business for Hilton luxury hotels globally
* Trusted partnerships built on long\-term value, credibility, and performance


**What will it be like to work for Hilton?**
 Hilton is the leading global hospitality company, spanning the lodging sector from luxurious full\-service hotels and resorts to extended\-stay suites and mid\-priced hotels. For more than a century, Hilton has offered business and leisure travelers the finest in accommodations, service, amenities and value. Hilton is dedicated to continuing its tradition of providing exceptional guest experiences across its global brands. Our vision “to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality” unites us as a team to create remarkable hospitality experiences around the world every day. And, our amazing Team Members are at the heart of it all!
   

  

**Work Locations**
 Hilton Hotels – Riyadh
   

  

**Schedule**
 Full\-time
   

  

**Brand**
 Hilton Corporate
   

  

**Job**
 Technology Operations

 Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented Senior Director \- FIDIC Engineer to join our team! In this role you will administer Contracts across the Master Developer Unit (MDU) Projects Portfolio, ensuring the works are delivered in full compliance with the Contracts' technical, contractual and performance requirements.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
* managing a team of resources on behalf of the Client for core services, including but not limited to, Contract Administration, Project Controls, Claims Management and Document Control in managing design development, coordination of construction services, administering commercial matters and payment certification.


**What Required Skills You'll Bring**
* \- Bachelor's Degree or equivalent work experience
* Typical Experience:
* 25\+ years experience within an Engineering, Contract or Commercial Management in major infrastructure projects.
* 10\+ years experience in a managerial role


**What Desired Skills You'll Bring**
* \- Advanced knowledge of forms of design and construction contracts including FIDIC or equivalent.
* Superb oral, written, and interpersonal communication skills
* Proactive Leadership
* Advanced presentation skills
* Ability to work well under pressure with strong attention to detail, making decisive decisions


 Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
   

  

 We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
   

  

 Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent\-recruitment/.

data.title: Enterprise Account Executive
data.raw_snippet:
Creatio is a global vendor of an AI\-native platform to automate workflows and CRM with no\-code and a maximum degree of freedom. Our platform combines an AI\-first architecture, composable no\-code tools, and enterprise\-grade governance to help organizations build and scale faster.
   

  

 We’re
data.jd_text:
Creatio is a global vendor of an AI\-native platform to automate workflows and CRM with no\-code and a maximum degree of freedom. Our platform combines an AI\-first architecture, composable no\-code tools, and enterprise\-grade governance to help organizations build and scale faster.
   

  

 We’re proud to be recognized by top industry analysts as a Leader and Strong Performer in multiple Gartner and Forrester reports.
   

  

 In 2025, Creatio was named to Inc.’s Best Workplaces list, recognizing our commitment to employee wellbeing and a strong workplace culture.
   

  

 For the role of
 **Enterprise Account Executive (KSA)** 
 , we are seeking an experienced field sales professional to help us scale the number of Enterprises that use the Creatio platform. Candidates must have experience building and developing a territory to high performance within an innovative SaaS technology provider. Successful candidates will help to build a portfolio of business within the top target enterprises in their territory. Successful candidates will need to drive their own activity and build a funnel while being supported by the broader Creatio organization of diverse and executive, product, and technical teams.
   

  

 The role is based in
 **Riyadh (KSA)** 
 and will report to the Regional Sales Director MEA (based in Dubai).
   

  

 Our targets are ambitious, and we are seeking engaging, collaborative, successful Enterprise Account Executives who have the skills, experience, and track record to make an immediate impact. We’re fast\-paced, innovative, and collaborative. We pursue excellence in everything and have a lot of fun along the way.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Deliver against revenue targets and all key performance metrics.
* Generate new business opportunities on an assigned territory, manage pipeline, opportunities, forecasting, and other items related to a normal outside sales function.
* Manage the end\-to\-end enterprise sales process and resource to create and execute winning solution sales strategies.
* Establish relationships with VP/C\-level buyers (Business and IT).
* Develop and orchestrate sales presentations and product demonstrations.
* Focus on new logo acquisition and manage relationships with the existing accounts to identify opportunities for cross and up\-sales.
* Ability to work with all customer functions including executive\-level personnel, various customer stakeholders, architecture, operations, design, vendor management, innovation, sourcing, and customer procurement.


**Desired Skills And Experience**
* Minimum 5 years of experience working within enterprise SaaS platform sales to large enterprises
* Proven track record of meeting quarterly and annual targets
* Deep understanding of executive\-level Enterprise and/or Global Account sales and methodologies
* Superb written and verbal communications and organizational skills
* 4\-year degree required, with technical curiosity and desire to learn
* Experience with CRM/BPM software space is strongly preferred
* Fluency in English and native Arabic


**What You Should Expect From Us**
* The award\-winning product (a Leader in Gartner Quadrants) to be proud of.
* Focus on making our clients successful (NPS score – 42%).
* Dedicated leadership with open doors.
* Steady stream of inbound leads (though we expect you to generate your own leads as well).
* Collaborative environment where everybody is happy to help.
* A remote\-first hybrid model: while giving plenty of space for concentration and personal working habits, we encourage regular meetings in one of our five hubs worldwide.
* Culture of genuine care, ownership, dedication, and high standards (learn more here).
* A vibrant corporate life: enjoy the opportunity to explore your teammates' cultures in online and offline events, participate in sports competitions, enjoy art master classes, and create your new favorite memories at our parties.
* Creatio offers all team members competitive pay.
* Paid leave options for life\-qualifying events, sicknesses, etc.

data.title: Senior Director - Central DMO - 20004885 CDU5
data.raw_snippet:
**Role Overview**
 The Senior Director \- Central DMO is responsible for establishing, leading, and optimizing centralized development management functions across the organization's portfolio. This role ensures consistency, efficiency, governance, and excellence in the planning and execution of deve
data.jd_text:
**Role Overview**
 The Senior Director \- Central DMO is responsible for establishing, leading, and optimizing centralized development management functions across the organization's portfolio. This role ensures consistency, efficiency, governance, and excellence in the planning and execution of development projects, from concept through delivery. The position plays a critical leadership role in aligning project execution with strategic objectives, operational standards, and financial targets
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Strategic Leadership
* Establish and lead the Central Development Management Office, defining its vision, structure, and operating model
* Align development management strategies with corporate goals and long\-term business plans
* Drive standardization of processes, tools, and methodologies across all development projects
* Act as a key advisor to executive leadership on development performance and risks
* Governance \& Oversight
* Implement governance frameworks to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to required quality standards
* Oversee project controls including scheduling, cost management, risk management, and reporting
* Ensure compliance with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices
* Lead stage\-gate review processes and investment committee inputs
* Portfolio \& Program Management
* Monitor and manage a portfolio of development projects across multiple asset classes
* Prioritize initiatives based on strategic importance, resource allocation, and ROI
* Identify and mitigate risks at portfolio and program levels
* Ensure effective coordination between projects to maximize efficiencies
* Process Excellence \& Continuous Improvement
* Develop and implement standardized development management frameworks, templates, and KPIs
* Drive digital transformation initiatives (PMIS, dashboards, reporting tools)
* Benchmark performance against industry standards and introduce best practices
* Lead lessons\-learned programs and continuous improvement initiatives
* Stakeholder Management
* Collaborate with internal departments including design, finance, procurement, legal, and operations
* Interface with external stakeholders such as consultants, contractors, and government authorities
* Ensure transparent communication and reporting to senior leadership and board\-level stakeholders
* Team Leadership
* Build, lead, and mentor a high\-performing team within the CDMO
* Define roles, responsibilities, and performance metrics for team members
* Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and excellence
* Drive talent development, succession planning, and capability building


**Requirements**
**Qualifications \& Experience**
* Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management, or related field (Master's preferred)
* 20\+ years of experience in real estate development, construction, or project management
* Proven track record in leading large\-scale, complex development portfolios
* Experience in establishing or managing PMOs/CDMOs or similar centralized functions
* Strong understanding of project management methodologies (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2 preferred)


**Skills \& Competencies**
* Strategic thinking and leadership capability
* Strong governance and project controls expertise
* Financial acumen and commercial awareness
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
* Change management and organizational transformation experience
* Ability to manage complexity and drive results in a fast\-paced environment


**Benefits**
 Competitive benefits package

data.title: Performance Improvement - Management Consultant
data.raw_snippet: At AlixPartners, we solve the most complex and critical challenges by moving quickly from analysis to action when it really matters; creating value that has a lasting impact on companies, their people, and the communities they serve. By understanding, respecting, and honouring the needs of our emplo
data.jd_text:
At AlixPartners, we solve the most complex and critical challenges by moving quickly from analysis to action when it really matters; creating value that has a lasting impact on companies, their people, and the communities they serve. By understanding, respecting, and honouring the needs of our employees, clients, and communities, AlixPartners actively promotes an inclusive environment. We strongly believe in the value that diversity brings to our experiences and are committed to the perpetual enhancements of initiatives, policies, and practices. We hold ourselves accountable by providing the space for authenticity, growth, and equity for everyone.
   

  

 AlixPartners has embraced a hybrid work model to provide flexibility and support our employees’ work\-life integration. Our hybrid model combines a mix of in\-person (at client site or AlixPartners office) and remote working. Travel is part of this position, but the frequency may vary based on client, team, and individual circumstances.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 The Performance Improvement practice is experiencing significant growth. Here you will be able to seize a unique opportunity to truly develop your long\-term career objectives. Our clients are top\-tier, household brands from a diverse range of sectors. We also advise a number of the leading private equity firms, supporting them on accelerating value creation within their portfolio companies, as well as on transaction\-related matters.
   

  

 This Vice President role is a permanent position located in Riyadh and reports to a Director.
   

  

* A crucial component of our specialist teams working on projects across a range of industries including Retail, Energy, Real Estate, Investment Funds, Aerospace \& Defence, etc.
* Support end\-to\-end delivery of performance improvement projects, including operational diagnostics, supply\-chain optimisation, cost\-reduction initiatives, and financial modelling to quantify impact and support decision\-making


 This description is not designed to encompass a comprehensive listing of required activities, duties or responsibilities.
   

  

**What You’ll Need**
* Master’s degree with evidence of strong academic success. Degree(s) in Business, Finance, Engineering (or foreign equivalent) preferable
* 5\-8 years of Consulting experience in a top tier international Consulting firm
* A broad range of functional expertise across supply chain, large transformations, financial modelling and pricing
* Strong knowledge of Excel and PowerPoint
* Willingness to travel across the GCC for client engagements
* You will hold valid residency for Saudi Arabia, or be able to obtain such, (administrative support will be provided by AlixPartners to obtain permits); ability to obtain work permit to travel and work across EU
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, Arabic is advantageous
* Willingness to work outside of normal business hours, and in particular as unique projects/needs arise.
* Ability to work full time in an office and remote environment; physically able to sit/stand at a computer and work in front of a computer screen for significant portions of the workday
* Must become familiar with, and promote and abide by, our Core Values as defined by the AlixPartners’ Code of Conduct and foster an inclusive environment with people at all levels of an organisation


 Office Location: KAFD, Riyadh
   

  

 Working hours: 8:30am – 5:30pm, Sunday to Thursday, hybrid model
   

  

*The firm offers market\-leading benefits that provide flexible options to support our employee’s needs, you can read more about the AlixPartners benefits here.*
*This advert will be removed once a suitable candidate has been identified.*
*AlixPartners is an equal opportunity employer.*
*\#* 
*LI\-Hybrid*
